revision.comment: Programmatically generating so that if this gets imported to wikipedia as a draft, the bot will attribute it to Immanuelle and send them g6 warnings

{{Infobox religious building | name = Tanabata-jinja
七夕神社 | image = Tanabata-jinja, Ogori, Fukuoka_03.jpg | caption = The ''[[honden]]'', or main shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|33|23|22.09|N|130|33|47.52|E|region:JP-40_type:landmark_source:kolossus-dewiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[The Cowherd and the Weaver Girl|Orihime]]
[[Himekoso-no-Kami]] | founded_by = | established = Unknown | date_destroyed = | location = 1-1, Ōsaki, [[Ogōri, Fukuoka|Ogōri]]
[[Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka]] 838-0127 | website = | architecture_style = | festival = 7 August | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Tanabata-jinja'''|七夕神社|}}, also known as Himekoso-jinja (媛社神社), is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ogōri, Fukuoka|Ogōri]], [[Fukuoka prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is dedicated to Orihime, the Japanese name of the Weaver Girl from the Chinese folk tale [[The Cowherd and the Weaver Girl|The Weaver Girl and the Cowherd]].{{cite web|url=https://goraifuku.jp/spots/detail/18|script-title=ja:媛社神社（七夕神社）|trans-title=Himekoso-jinja|accessdate=September 18, 2019|publisher=[[Fukuoka Prefecture]]|language=Japanese}} The weaver is celebrated in [[Tanabata]], a Japanese festival. ==History== According to the Hizen-Fudoki, an ancient record of the [[Hizen Province]], this shrine was extant in 730. The original object of worship at the shrine was a river, Yamaji-gawa, on the south of the shrine. In ''[[Engishiki]]'', a formal record on shrines written 1000 years ago, this area was developed by the textile industry. The villagers believe the weaving goddess known as ''Tanabata-tsume'' (棚機津女) from ancient times, and the story of the Cowherd and the Weaver Girl from [[China]] was assimilated, it is now known as the god of textiles. ==Festivals== The Tanabata Grand festival takes place every year on August 7.{{cite web|url=http://ogorishinorekishi.ogori.net/小郡市の歴史遺産/七夕神社/|script-title=ja:小郡市の歴史遺産 七夕神社|trans-title=Tanabata-jinja, the historical heritage of Ogōri city|accessdate=September 18, 2019|publisher=[[ogori city]]|language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== File:Tanabata-jinja, Ogori, Fukuoka 01.jpg|Torii at Tanabata Shrine entrance. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== *[https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/en/event/?mode=detail&id=4000000000656 Tanabata Shrine - Fukuoka Prefecture Tourist Information] {{en icon}} {{Commons category|Tanabata-jinja_(Ogori)|Tanabata Shrine}} {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Tourist attractions in Fukuoka Prefecture]] {{Shinto-stub}}

{{Infobox religious building | name = Tanabata-jinja

[[Himekoso-no-Kami]] | founded_by = | established = Unknown | date_destroyed = | location = 1-1, Ōsaki, [[Ogōri, Fukuoka|Ogōri]]
[[Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka]] 838-0127 | website = | architecture_style = | festival = 7 August | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Tanabata-jinja'''|七夕神社|}}, also known as Himekoso-jinja (媛社神社), is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ogōri, Fukuoka|Ogōri]], [[Fukuoka prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is dedicated to Orihime, the Japanese name of the Weaver Girl from the Chinese folk tale [[The Cowherd and the Weaver Girl|The Weaver Girl and the Cowherd]].{{cite web|url=https://goraifuku.jp/spots/detail/18|script-title=ja:媛社神社（七夕神社）|trans-title=Himekoso-jinja|accessdate=September 18, 2019|publisher=[[Fukuoka Prefecture]]|language=Japanese}} The weaver is celebrated in [[Tanabata]], a Japanese festival. ==History== According to the Hizen-Fudoki, an ancient record of the [[Hizen Province]], this shrine was extant in 730. The original object of worship at the shrine was a river, Yamaji-gawa, on the south of the shrine. In ''[[Engishiki]]'', a formal record on shrines written 1000 years ago, this area was developed by the textile industry. The villagers believe the weaving goddess known as ''Tanabata-tsume'' (棚機津女) from ancient times, and the story of the Cowherd and the Weaver Girl from [[China]] was assimilated, it is now known as the god of textiles. ==Festivals== The Tanabata Grand festival takes place every year on August 7.{{cite web|url=http://ogorishinorekishi.ogori.net/小郡市の歴史遺産/七夕神社/|script-title=ja:小郡市の歴史遺産 七夕神社|trans-title=Tanabata-jinja, the historical heritage of Ogōri city|accessdate=September 18, 2019|publisher=[[ogori city]]|language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== File:Tanabata-jinja, Ogori, Fukuoka 01.jpg|Torii at Tanabata Shrine entrance. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== *[https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/en/event/?mode=detail&id=4000000000656 Tanabata Shrine - Fukuoka Prefecture Tourist Information] {{en icon}} {{Commons category|Tanabata-jinja_(Ogori)|Tanabata Shrine}} {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Tourist attractions in Fukuoka Prefecture]] {{Shinto-stub}}

{{Infobox religious building | name = Tanabata-jinja
七夕神社 | image = Tanabata-jinja, Ogori, Fukuoka_03.jpg | caption = The ''[[honden]]'', or main shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|33|23|22.09|N|130|33|47.52|E|region:JP-40_type:landmark_source:kolossus-dewiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[The Cowherd and the Weaver Girl|Orihime]]
[[Himekoso-no-Kami]] | founded_by = | established = Unknown | date_destroyed = | location = 1-1, Ōsaki, [[Ogōri, Fukuoka|Ogōri]]
[[Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka]] 838-0127 | website = | architecture_style = | festival = 7 August | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Tanabata-jinja'''|七夕神社|}}, also known as Himekoso-jinja (媛社神社), is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ogōri, Fukuoka|Ogōri]], [[Fukuoka prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is dedicated to Orihime, the Japanese name of the Weaver Girl from the Chinese folk tale [[The Cowherd and the Weaver Girl|The Weaver Girl and the Cowherd]].{{cite web|url=https://goraifuku.jp/spots/detail/18|script-title=ja:媛社神社（七夕神社）|trans-title=Himekoso-jinja|accessdate=September 18, 2019|publisher=[[Fukuoka Prefecture]]|language=Japanese}} The weaver is celebrated in [[Tanabata]], a Japanese festival. ==History== According to the Hizen-Fudoki, an ancient record of the [[Hizen Province]], this shrine was extant in 730. The original object of worship at the shrine was a river, Yamaji-gawa, on the south of the shrine. In ''[[Engishiki]]'', a formal record on shrines written 1000 years ago, this area was developed by the textile industry. The villagers believe the weaving goddess known as ''Tanabata-tsume'' (棚機津女) from ancient times, and the story of the Cowherd and the Weaver Girl from [[China]] was assimilated, it is now known as the god of textiles. ==Festivals== Tanabata Grand festival, the annual summer festival held August 6-8 every year. On the evening of August 6 children bring the ''[[mikoshi]]'' from the shrine, carry it around the neighborhoods that worship at the shrine. At 5 o'clock on the following day morning, the lion dance is performed as a prayer dedicated to the shrine. And later in the morning, the lion dancer and children pulling a float visit neighbors. In the evening, many open-air stalls line the path leading to the shrine, and wishes written on strips of paper sent from people throughout Japan decorate every available space.{{cite web |url=https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/en/event/?mode=detail&id=4000000002698&isSpot=&isEvent=1|title=Summer Festival at Tanabata Shrine|publisher=[[Fukuoka Prefecture]] |accessdate=19 September 2019}} ==Gallery== File:Tanabata-jinja, Ogori, Fukuoka 01.jpg|Torii at Tanabata Shrine entrance. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== *[https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/en/event/?mode=detail&id=4000000000656 Tanabata Shrine - Fukuoka Prefecture Tourist Information] {{en icon}} {{Commons category|Tanabata-jinja_(Ogori)|Tanabata Shrine}} {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Tourist attractions in Fukuoka Prefecture]] {{Shinto-stub}}

In the evening, many open-air stalls line the path leading to the shrine, and wishes written on strips of paper sent from people throughout Japan decorate every available space.{{cite web |url=https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/en/event/?mode=detail&id=4000000002698&isSpot=&isEvent=1|title=Summer Festival at Tanabata Shrine|publisher=[[Fukuoka Prefecture]] |accessdate=19 September 2019}} ==Gallery== File:Tanabata-jinja, Ogori, Fukuoka 01.jpg|Torii at Tanabata Shrine entrance. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== *[https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/en/event/?mode=detail&id=4000000000656 Tanabata Shrine - Fukuoka Prefecture Tourist Information] {{en icon}} {{Commons category|Tanabata-jinja_(Fukuoka)|Tanabata Shrine}} {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Tourist attractions in Fukuoka Prefecture]] {{Shinto-stub}}

The villagers believe the weaving goddess known as ''Tanabata-tsume'' (棚機津女) from ancient times, and the story of the Cowherd and the Weaver Girl from [[China]] was assimilated, it is now known as the god of textiles. ==Festivals== Tanabata Grand festival, the annual summer festival held August 6-8 every year.{{cite web|url=http://ogorishinorekishi.ogori.net/小郡市の歴史遺産/七夕神社/|script-title=ja:小郡市の歴史遺産 七夕神社|trans-title=Tanabata-jinja, the historical site in Ogōri city|accessdate=September 18, 2019|publisher=[[ogori city]]|language=Japanese}} On the evening of August 6 children bring the ''[[mikoshi]]'' from the shrine, carry it around the neighborhoods that worship at the shrine. At 5 o'clock on the following day morning, the lion dance is performed as a prayer dedicated to the shrine. And later in the morning, the lion dancer and children pulling a float visit neighbors. In the evening, many open-air stalls line the path leading to the shrine, and wishes written on strips of paper sent from people throughout Japan decorate every available space.{{cite web |url=https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/en/event/?mode=detail&id=4000000002698&isSpot=&isEvent=1|title=Summer Festival at Tanabata Shrine|publisher=[[Fukuoka Prefecture]] |accessdate=19 September 2019}} ==Gallery== File:Tanabata-jinja, Ogori, Fukuoka 01.jpg|Torii at Tanabata Shrine entrance. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== *[https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/en/event/?mode=detail&id=4000000000656 Tanabata Shrine - Fukuoka Prefecture Tourist Information] {{in lang|en}} {{Commons category:new pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category|Tanabata-jinja_(Fukuoka)|Tanabata Shrine}} {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:new pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Tourist attractions in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Completed pages from wikipedia]] [[Category:kk]] {{Shinto-stub}}

{{Infobox religious building | name = Tanabata-jinja
七夕神社 | image = Tanabata-jinja, Ogori, Fukuoka_03.jpg | caption = The ''[[honden]]'', or main shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|33|23|22.09|N|130|33|47.52|E|region:JP-40_type:landmark_source:kolossus-dewiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[The Cowherd and the Weaver Girl|Orihime]]
[[Himekoso-no-Kami]] | founded_by = | established = Unknown | date_destroyed = | location = 1-1, Ōzaki, [[Ogōri, Fukuoka|Ogōri]]
[[Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka]] 838-0127 | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Tanabata-jinja'''|七夕神社|}}, also known as Himekoso-jinja (媛社神社), is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ogōri, Fukuoka|Ogōri]], [[Fukuoka prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is dedicated to Orihime, the Japanese name of the Weaver Girl from the Chinese folk tale [[The Cowherd and the Weaver Girl|The Weaver Girl and the Cowherd]].{{cite web|url=https://goraifuku.jp/spots/detail/18|script-title=ja:媛社神社（七夕神社）|trans-title=Himekoso-jinja|accessdate=September 18, 2019|publisher=[[Fukuoka Prefecture]]|language=Japanese}} The weaver is celebrated in [[Tanabata]], a Japanese festival. ==History== According to the Hizen-Fudoki, an ancient record of the [[Hizen Province]], this shrine was extant in 730. The original object of worship at the shrine was a river, Yamaji-gawa, on the south of the shrine. In ''[[Engishiki]]'', a formal record on shrines written 1000 years ago, this area was developed by the textile industry. The villagers believe the weaving goddess known as ''Tanabata-tsume'' (棚機津女) from ancient times, and the story of the Cowherd and the Weaver Girl from [[China]] was assimilated, it is now known as the god of textiles. ==Festivals== Tanabata Grand festival, the annual summer festival held August 6-8 every year.{{cite web|url=http://ogorishinorekishi.ogori.net/小郡市の歴史遺産/七夕神社/|script-title=ja:小郡市の歴史遺産 七夕神社|trans-title=Tanabata-jinja, the historical site in Ogōri city|accessdate=September 18, 2019|publisher=[[ogori city]]|language=Japanese}} On the evening of August 6 children bring the ''[[mikoshi]]'' from the shrine, carry it around the neighborhoods that worship at the shrine. At 5 o'clock on the following day morning, the lion dance is performed as a prayer dedicated to the shrine. And later in the morning, the lion dancer and children pulling a float visit neighbors. In the evening, many open-air stalls line the path leading to the shrine, and wishes written on strips of paper sent from people throughout Japan decorate every available space.{{cite web |url=https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/en/event/?mode=detail&id=4000000002698&isSpot=&isEvent=1|title=Summer Festival at Tanabata Shrine|publisher=[[Fukuoka Prefecture]] |accessdate=19 September 2019}} revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building | name = Tanabata-jinja
七夕神社 | image = Tanabata-jinja, Ogori, Fukuoka_03.jpg | caption = The ''[[honden]]'', or main shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|33|23|22.09|N|130|33|47.52|E|region:JP-40_type:landmark_source:kolossus-dewiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[The Cowherd and the Weaver Girl|Orihime]]
[[Himekoso-no-Kami]] | founded_by = | established = Unknown | date_destroyed = | location = 1-1, Ōzaki, [[Ogōri, Fukuoka|Ogōri]]
[[Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka]] 838-0127 | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Tanabata-jinja'''|七夕神社|}}, also known as Himekoso-jinja (媛社神社), is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ogōri, Fukuoka|Ogōri]], [[Fukuoka prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is dedicated to Orihime, the Japanese name of the Weaver Girl from the Chinese folk tale [[The Cowherd and the Weaver Girl|The Weaver Girl and the Cowherd]].{{cite web|url=https://goraifuku.jp/spots/detail/18|script-title=ja:媛社神社（七夕神社）|trans-title=Himekoso-jinja|accessdate=September 18, 2019|publisher=[[Fukuoka Prefecture]]|language=Japanese}} The weaver is celebrated in [[Tanabata]], a Japanese festival. ==History== According to the Hizen-Fudoki, an ancient record of the [[Hizen Province]], this shrine was extant in 730. The original object of worship at the shrine was a river, Yamaji-gawa, on the south of the shrine. In ''[[Engishiki]]'', a formal record on shrines written 1000 years ago, this area was developed by the textile industry. The villagers believe the weaving goddess known as ''Tanabata-tsume'' (棚機津女) from ancient times, and the story of the Cowherd and the Weaver Girl from [[China]] was assimilated, it is now known as the god of textiles. ==Festivals== Tanabata Grand festival, the annual summer festival held August 6-8 every year.{{cite web|url=http://ogorishinorekishi.ogori.net/小郡市の歴史遺産/七夕神社/|script-title=ja:小郡市の歴史遺産 七夕神社|trans-title=Tanabata-jinja, the historical site in Ogōri city|accessdate=September 18, 2019|publisher=[[ogori city]]|language=Japanese}} On the evening of August 6 children bring the ''[[mikoshi]]'' from the shrine, carry it around the neighborhoods that worship at the shrine. At 5 o'clock on the following day morning, the lion dance is performed as a prayer dedicated to the shrine. And later in the morning, the lion dancer and children pulling a float visit neighbors. In the evening, many open-air stalls line the path leading to the shrine, and wishes written on strips of paper sent from people throughout Japan decorate every available space.{{cite web |url=https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/en/event/?mode=detail&id=4000000002698&isSpot=&isEvent=1|title=Summer Festival at Tanabata Shrine|publisher=[[Fukuoka Prefecture]] |accessdate=19 September 2019}} ==Gallery== File:Tanabata-jinja, Ogori, Fukuoka 01.jpg|Torii at Tanabata Shrine entrance. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== [[Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka]] 838-0127 | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Tanabata-jinja'''|七夕神社|}}, also known as Himekoso-jinja (媛社神社), is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ogōri, Fukuoka|Ogōri]], [[Fukuoka prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is dedicated to Orihime, the Japanese name of the Weaver Girl from the Chinese folk tale [[The Cowherd and the Weaver Girl|The Weaver Girl and the Cowherd]].{{cite web|url=https://goraifuku.jp/spots/detail/18|script-title=ja:媛社神社（七夕神社）|trans-title=Himekoso-jinja|accessdate=September 18, 2019|publisher=[[Fukuoka Prefecture]]|language=Japanese}} The weaver is celebrated in [[Tanabata]], a Japanese festival. ==History== According to the Hizen-Fudoki, an ancient record of the [[Hizen Province]], this shrine was extant in 730. The original object of worship at the shrine was a river, Yamaji-gawa, on the south of the shrine. In ''[[Engishiki]]'', a formal record on shrines written 1000 years ago, this area was developed by the textile industry. The villagers believe the weaving goddess known as ''Tanabata-tsume'' (棚機津女) from ancient times, and the story of the Cowherd and the Weaver Girl from [[China]] was assimilated, it is now known as the god of textiles. ==Festivals== Tanabata Grand festival, the annual summer festival held August 6-8 every year.{{cite web|url=http://ogorishinorekishi.ogori.net/小郡市の歴史遺産/七夕神社/|script-title=ja:小郡市の歴史遺産 七夕神社|trans-title=Tanabata-jinja, the historical site in Ogōri city|accessdate=September 18, 2019|publisher=[[ogori city]]|language=Japanese}} On the evening of August 6 children bring the ''[[mikoshi]]'' from the shrine, carry it around the neighborhoods that worship at the shrine. At 5 o'clock on the following day morning, the lion dance is performed as a prayer dedicated to the shrine. And later in the morning, the lion dancer and children pulling a float visit neighbors. In the evening, many open-air stalls line the path leading to the shrine, and wishes written on strips of paper sent from people throughout Japan decorate every available space.{{cite web |url=https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/en/event/?mode=detail&id=4000000002698&isSpot=&isEvent=1|title=Summer Festival at Tanabata Shrine|publisher=[[Fukuoka Prefecture]] |accessdate=19 September 2019}} ==Gallery== File:Tanabata-jinja, Ogori, Fukuoka 01.jpg|Torii at Tanabata Shrine entrance. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building | name = Tanabata-jinja
七夕神社 | image = Tanabata-jinja, Ogori, Fukuoka_03.jpg | caption = The ''[[honden]]'', or main shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|33|23|22.09|N|130|33|47.52|E|region:JP-40_type:landmark_source:kolossus-dewiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[The Cowherd and the Weaver Girl|Orihime]]
[[Himekoso-no-Kami]] | founded_by = | established = Unknown | date_destroyed = | location = 1-1, Ōzaki, [[Ogōri, Fukuoka|Ogōri]]
[[Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka]] 838-0127 | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Tanabata-jinja'''|七夕神社|}}, also known as Himekoso-jinja (媛社神社), is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ogōri, Fukuoka|Ogōri]], [[Fukuoka prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is dedicated to Orihime, the Japanese name of the Weaver Girl from the Chinese folk tale [[The Cowherd and the Weaver Girl|The Weaver Girl and the Cowherd]].{{cite web|url=https://goraifuku.jp/spots/detail/18|script-title=ja:媛社神社（七夕神社）|trans-title=Himekoso-jinja|accessdate=September 18, 2019|publisher=[[Fukuoka Prefecture]]|language=Japanese}} The weaver is celebrated in [[Tanabata]], a Japanese festival. ==History== According to the Hizen-Fudoki, an ancient record of the [[Hizen Province]], this shrine was extant in 730. The original object of worship at the shrine was a river, Yamaji-gawa, on the south of the shrine. In ''[[Engishiki]]'', a formal record on shrines written 1000 years ago, this area was developed by the textile industry. The villagers believe the weaving goddess known as ''Tanabata-tsume'' (棚機津女) from ancient times, and the story of the Cowherd and the Weaver Girl from [[China]] was assimilated, it is now known as the god of textiles. ==Festivals== Tanabata Grand festival, the annual summer festival held August 6-8 every year.{{cite web|url=http://ogorishinorekishi.ogori.net/小郡市の歴史遺産/七夕神社/|script-title=ja:小郡市の歴史遺産 七夕神社|trans-title=Tanabata-jinja, the historical site in Ogōri city|accessdate=September 18, 2019|publisher=[[ogori city]]|language=Japanese}} On the evening of August 6 children bring the ''[[mikoshi]]'' from the shrine, carry it around the neighborhoods that worship at the shrine. At 5 o'clock on the following day morning, the lion dance is performed as a prayer dedicated to the shrine. And later in the morning, the lion dancer and children pulling a float visit neighbors. In the evening, many open-air stalls line the path leading to the shrine, and wishes written on strips of paper sent from people throughout Japan decorate every available space.{{cite web |url=https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/en/event/?mode=detail&id=4000000002698&isSpot=&isEvent=1|title=Summer Festival at Tanabata Shrine|publisher=[[Fukuoka Prefecture]] |accessdate=19 September 2019}} ==Gallery== File:Tanabata-jinja, Ogori, Fukuoka 01.jpg|Torii at Tanabata Shrine entrance. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== [[Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka]] 838-0127 | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Tanabata-jinja'''|七夕神社|}}, also known as Himekoso-jinja (媛社神社), is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ogōri, Fukuoka|Ogōri]], [[Fukuoka prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is dedicated to Orihime, the Japanese name of the Weaver Girl from the Chinese folk tale [[The Cowherd and the Weaver Girl|The Weaver Girl and the Cowherd]].{{cite web|url=https://goraifuku.jp/spots/detail/18|script-title=ja:媛社神社（七夕神社）|trans-title=Himekoso-jinja|accessdate=September 18, 2019|publisher=[[Fukuoka Prefecture]]|language=Japanese}} The weaver is celebrated in [[Tanabata]], a Japanese festival. ==History== According to the Hizen-Fudoki, an ancient record of the [[Hizen Province]], this shrine was extant in 730. The original object of worship at the shrine was a river, Yamaji-gawa, on the south of the shrine. In ''[[Engishiki]]'', a formal record on shrines written 1000 years ago, this area was developed by the textile industry. The villagers believe the weaving goddess known as ''Tanabata-tsume'' (棚機津女) from ancient times, and the story of the Cowherd and the Weaver Girl from [[China]] was assimilated, it is now known as the god of textiles. ==Festivals== Tanabata Grand festival, the annual summer festival held August 6-8 every year.{{cite web|url=http://ogorishinorekishi.ogori.net/小郡市の歴史遺産/七夕神社/|script-title=ja:小郡市の歴史遺産 七夕神社|trans-title=Tanabata-jinja, the historical site in Ogōri city|accessdate=September 18, 2019|publisher=[[ogori city]]|language=Japanese}} On the evening of August 6 children bring the ''[[mikoshi]]'' from the shrine, carry it around the neighborhoods that worship at the shrine. At 5 o'clock on the following day morning, the lion dance is performed as a prayer dedicated to the shrine. And later in the morning, the lion dancer and children pulling a float visit neighbors. In the evening, many open-air stalls line the path leading to the shrine, and wishes written on strips of paper sent from people throughout Japan decorate every available space.{{cite web |url=https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/en/event/?mode=detail&id=4000000002698&isSpot=&isEvent=1|title=Summer Festival at Tanabata Shrine|publisher=[[Fukuoka Prefecture]] |accessdate=19 September 2019}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Tanabata-jinja
七夕神社 | image = Tanabata-jinja, Ogori, Fukuoka_03.jpg | caption = The ''[[honden]]'', or main shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|33|23|22.09|N|130|33|47.52|E|region:JP-40_type:landmark_source:kolossus-dewiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[The Cowherd and the Weaver Girl|Orihime]]
[[Himekoso-no-Kami]] | founded_by = | established = Unknown | date_destroyed = | location = 1-1, Ōzaki, [[Ogōri, Fukuoka|Ogōri]]
[[Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka]] 838-0127 | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Tanabata-jinja'''|七夕神社|}}, also known as Himekoso-jinja (媛社神社), is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ogōri, Fukuoka|Ogōri]], [[Fukuoka prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is dedicated to Orihime, the Japanese name of the Weaver Girl from the Chinese folk tale [[The Cowherd and the Weaver Girl|The Weaver Girl and the Cowherd]].{{cite web|url=https://goraifuku.jp/spots/detail/18|script-title=ja:媛社神社（七夕神社）|trans-title=Himekoso-jinja|accessdate=September 18, 2019|publisher=[[Fukuoka Prefecture]]|language=Japanese}} The weaver is celebrated in [[Tanabata]], a Japanese festival. ==History== According to the Hizen-Fudoki, an ancient record of the [[Hizen Province]], this shrine was extant in 730. The original object of worship at the shrine was a river, Yamaji-gawa, on the south of the shrine. In ''[[Engishiki]]'', a formal record on shrines written 1000 years ago, this area was developed by the textile industry. The villagers believe the weaving goddess known as ''Tanabata-tsume'' (棚機津女) from ancient times, and the story of the Cowherd and the Weaver Girl from [[China]] was assimilated, it is now known as the god of textiles. ==Festivals== Tanabata Grand festival, the annual summer festival held August 6-8 every year.{{cite web|url=http://ogorishinorekishi.ogori.net/小郡市の歴史遺産/七夕神社/|script-title=ja:小郡市の歴史遺産 七夕神社|trans-title=Tanabata-jinja, the historical site in Ogōri city|accessdate=September 18, 2019|publisher=[[ogori city]]|language=Japanese}} On the evening of August 6 children bring the ''[[mikoshi]]'' from the shrine, carry it around the neighborhoods that worship at the shrine. At 5 o'clock on the following day morning, the lion dance is performed as a prayer dedicated to the shrine. And later in the morning, the lion dancer and children pulling a float visit neighbors. In the evening, many open-air stalls line the path leading to the shrine, and wishes written on strips of paper sent from people throughout Japan decorate every available space.{{cite web |url=https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/en/event/?mode=detail&id=4000000002698&isSpot=&isEvent=1|title=Summer Festival at Tanabata Shrine|publisher=[[Fukuoka Prefecture]] |accessdate=19 September 2019}} ==Gallery== File:Tanabata-jinja, Ogori, Fukuoka 01.jpg|Torii at Tanabata Shrine entrance. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== 七夕神社 | image = Tanabata-jinja, Ogori, Fukuoka_03.jpg | caption = The ''[[honden]]'', or main shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|33|23|22.09|N|130|33|47.52|E|region:JP-40_type:landmark_source:kolossus-dewiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[The Cowherd and the Weaver Girl|Orihime]]
[[Himekoso-no-Kami]] | founded_by = | established = Unknown | date_destroyed = | location = 1-1, Ōzaki, [[Ogōri, Fukuoka|Ogōri]]
[[Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka]] 838-0127 | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Tanabata-jinja'''|七夕神社|}}, also known as Himekoso-jinja (媛社神社), is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ogōri, Fukuoka|Ogōri]], [[Fukuoka prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is dedicated to Orihime, the Japanese name of the Weaver Girl from the Chinese folk tale [[The Cowherd and the Weaver Girl|The Weaver Girl and the Cowherd]].{{cite web|url=https://goraifuku.jp/spots/detail/18|script-title=ja:媛社神社（七夕神社）|trans-title=Himekoso-jinja|accessdate=September 18, 2019|publisher=[[Fukuoka Prefecture]]|language=Japanese}} The weaver is celebrated in [[Tanabata]], a Japanese festival. ==History== According to the Hizen-Fudoki, an ancient record of the [[Hizen Province]], this shrine was extant in 730. The original object of worship at the shrine was a river, Yamaji-gawa, on the south of the shrine. In ''[[Engishiki]]'', a formal record on shrines written 1000 years ago, this area was developed by the textile industry. The villagers believe the weaving goddess known as ''Tanabata-tsume'' (棚機津女) from ancient times, and the story of the Cowherd and the Weaver Girl from [[China]] was assimilated, it is now known as the god of textiles. ==Festivals== Tanabata Grand festival, the annual summer festival held August 6-8 every year.{{cite web|url=http://ogorishinorekishi.ogori.net/小郡市の歴史遺産/七夕神社/|script-title=ja:小郡市の歴史遺産 七夕神社|trans-title=Tanabata-jinja, the historical site in Ogōri city|accessdate=September 18, 2019|publisher=[[ogori city]]|language=Japanese}} On the evening of August 6 children bring the ''[[mikoshi]]'' from the shrine, carry it around the neighborhoods that worship at the shrine. At 5 o'clock on the following day morning, the lion dance is performed as a prayer dedicated to the shrine. And later in the morning, the lion dancer and children pulling a float visit neighbors. In the evening, many open-air stalls line the path leading to the shrine, and wishes written on strips of paper sent from people throughout Japan decorate every available space.{{cite web |url=https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/en/event/?mode=detail&id=4000000002698&isSpot=&isEvent=1|title=Summer Festival at Tanabata Shrine|publisher=[[Fukuoka Prefecture]] |accessdate=19 September 2019}} ==Gallery== File:Tanabata-jinja, Ogori, Fukuoka 01.jpg|Torii at Tanabata Shrine entrance. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Infobox religious building | name = Tanabata-jinja
七夕神社 | image = Tanabata-jinja, Ogori, Fukuoka_03.jpg | caption = The ''[[honden]]'', or main shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|33|23|22.09|N|130|33|47.52|E|region:JP-40_type:landmark_source:kolossus-dewiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[The Cowherd and the Weaver Girl|Orihime]]
[[Himekoso-no-Kami]] | founded_by = | established = Unknown | date_destroyed = | location = 1-1, Ōzaki, [[Ogōri, Fukuoka|Ogōri]]
[[Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka]] 838-0127 | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Tanabata-jinja'''|七夕神社|}}, also known as Himekoso-jinja (媛社神社), is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ogōri, Fukuoka|Ogōri]], [[Fukuoka prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is dedicated to Orihime, the Japanese name of the Weaver Girl from the Chinese folk tale [[The Cowherd and the Weaver Girl|The Weaver Girl and the Cowherd]].{{cite web|url=https://goraifuku.jp/spots/detail/18|script-title=ja:媛社神社（七夕神社）|trans-title=Himekoso-jinja|accessdate=September 18, 2019|publisher=[[Fukuoka Prefecture]]|language=Japanese}} The weaver is celebrated in [[Tanabata]], a Japanese festival. ==History== According to the Hizen-Fudoki, an ancient record of the [[Hizen Province]], this shrine was extant in 730. The original object of worship at the shrine was a river, Yamaji-gawa, on the south of the shrine. In ''[[Engishiki]]'', a formal record on shrines written 1000 years ago, this area was developed by the textile industry. The villagers believe the weaving goddess known as ''Tanabata-tsume'' (棚機津女) from ancient times, and the story of the Cowherd and the Weaver Girl from [[China]] was assimilated, it is now known as the god of textiles. ==Festivals== Tanabata Grand festival, the annual summer festival held August 6-8 every year.{{cite web|url=http://ogorishinorekishi.ogori.net/小郡市の歴史遺産/七夕神社/|script-title=ja:小郡市の歴史遺産 七夕神社|trans-title=Tanabata-jinja, the historical site in Ogōri city|accessdate=September 18, 2019|publisher=[[ogori city]]|language=Japanese}} On the evening of August 6 children bring the ''[[mikoshi]]'' from the shrine, carry it around the neighborhoods that worship at the shrine. At 5 o'clock on the following day morning, the lion dance is performed as a prayer dedicated to the shrine. And later in the morning, the lion dancer and children pulling a float visit neighbors. In the evening, many open-air stalls line the path leading to the shrine, and wishes written on strips of paper sent from people throughout Japan decorate every available space.{{cite web |url=https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/en/event/?mode=detail&id=4000000002698&isSpot=&isEvent=1|title=Summer Festival at Tanabata Shrine|publisher=[[Fukuoka Prefecture]] |accessdate=19 September 2019}} ==Gallery== File:Tanabata-jinja, Ogori, Fukuoka 01.jpg|Torii at Tanabata Shrine entrance. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== 七夕神社 | image = Tanabata-jinja, Ogori, Fukuoka_03.jpg | caption = The ''[[honden]]'', or main shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|33|23|22.09|N|130|33|47.52|E|region:JP-40_type:landmark_source:kolossus-dewiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[The Cowherd and the Weaver Girl|Orihime]]
[[Himekoso-no-Kami]] | founded_by = | established = Unknown | date_destroyed = | location = 1-1, Ōzaki, [[Ogōri, Fukuoka|Ogōri]]
[[Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka]] 838-0127 | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Tanabata-jinja'''|七夕神社|}}, also known as Himekoso-jinja (媛社神社), is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ogōri, Fukuoka|Ogōri]], [[Fukuoka prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is dedicated to Orihime, the Japanese name of the Weaver Girl from the Chinese folk tale [[The Cowherd and the Weaver Girl|The Weaver Girl and the Cowherd]].{{cite web|url=https://goraifuku.jp/spots/detail/18|script-title=ja:媛社神社（七夕神社）|trans-title=Himekoso-jinja|accessdate=September 18, 2019|publisher=[[Fukuoka Prefecture]]|language=Japanese}} The weaver is celebrated in [[Tanabata]], a Japanese festival. ==History== According to the Hizen-Fudoki, an ancient record of the [[Hizen Province]], this shrine was extant in 730. The original object of worship at the shrine was a river, Yamaji-gawa, on the south of the shrine. In ''[[Engishiki]]'', a formal record on shrines written 1000 years ago, this area was developed by the textile industry. The villagers believe the weaving goddess known as ''Tanabata-tsume'' (棚機津女) from ancient times, and the story of the Cowherd and the Weaver Girl from [[China]] was assimilated, it is now known as the god of textiles. ==Festivals== Tanabata Grand festival, the annual summer festival held August 6-8 every year.{{cite web|url=http://ogorishinorekishi.ogori.net/小郡市の歴史遺産/七夕神社/|script-title=ja:小郡市の歴史遺産 七夕神社|trans-title=Tanabata-jinja, the historical site in Ogōri city|accessdate=September 18, 2019|publisher=[[ogori city]]|language=Japanese}} On the evening of August 6 children bring the ''[[mikoshi]]'' from the shrine, carry it around the neighborhoods that worship at the shrine. At 5 o'clock on the following day morning, the lion dance is performed as a prayer dedicated to the shrine. And later in the morning, the lion dancer and children pulling a float visit neighbors. In the evening, many open-air stalls line the path leading to the shrine, and wishes written on strips of paper sent from people throughout Japan decorate every available space.{{cite web |url=https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/en/event/?mode=detail&id=4000000002698&isSpot=&isEvent=1|title=Summer Festival at Tanabata Shrine|publisher=[[Fukuoka Prefecture]] |accessdate=19 September 2019}} The villagers believe the weaving goddess known as ''Tanabata-tsume'' (棚機津女) from ancient times, and the story of the Cowherd and the Weaver Girl from [[China]] was assimilated, it is now known as the god of textiles. ==Festivals== Tanabata Grand festival, the annual summer festival held August 6-8 every year.{{cite web|url=http://ogorishinorekishi.ogori.net/小郡市の歴史遺産/七夕神社/|script-title=ja:小郡市の歴史遺産 七夕神社|trans-title=Tanabata-jinja, the historical site in Ogōri city|accessdate=September 18, 2019|publisher=[[ogori city]]|language=Japanese}} On the evening of August 6 children bring the ''[[mikoshi]]'' from the shrine, carry it around the neighborhoods that worship at the shrine. At 5 o'clock on the following day morning, the lion dance is performed as a prayer dedicated to the shrine. And later in the morning, the lion dancer and children pulling a float visit neighbors. In the evening, many open-air stalls line the path leading to the shrine, and wishes written on strips of paper sent from people throughout Japan decorate every available space.{{cite web It is dedicated to Orihime, the Japanese name of the Weaver Girl from the Chinese folk tale [[The Cowherd and the Weaver Girl|The Weaver Girl and the Cowherd]].{{cite web|url=https://goraifuku.jp/spots/detail/18|script-title=ja:媛社神社（七夕神社）|trans-title=Himekoso-jinja|accessdate=September 18, 2019|publisher=[[Fukuoka Prefecture]]|language=Japanese}} The weaver is celebrated in [[Tanabata]], a Japanese festival. ==History== According to the Hizen-Fudoki, an ancient record of the [[Hizen Province]], this shrine was extant in 730. The original object of worship at the shrine was a river, Yamaji-gawa, on the south of the shrine. In ''[[Engishiki]]'', a formal record on shrines written 1000 years ago, this area was developed by the textile industry. The villagers believe the weaving goddess known as ''Tanabata-tsume'' (棚機津女) from ancient times, and the story of the Cowherd and the Weaver Girl from [[China]] was assimilated, it is now known as the god of textiles. ==Festivals== Tanabata Grand festival, the annual summer festival held August 6-8 every year.{{cite web|url=http://ogorishinorekishi.ogori.net/小郡市の歴史遺産/七夕神社/|script-title=ja:小郡市の歴史遺産 七夕神社|trans-title=Tanabata-jinja, the historical site in Ogōri city|accessdate=September 18, 2019|publisher=[[ogori city]]|language=Japanese}} On the evening of August 6 children bring the ''[[mikoshi]]'' from the shrine, carry it around the neighborhoods that worship at the shrine. At 5 o'clock on the following day morning, the lion dance is performed as a prayer dedicated to the shrine. And later in the morning, the lion dancer and children pulling a float visit neighbors. In the evening, many open-air stalls line the path leading to the shrine, and wishes written on strips of paper sent from people throughout Japan decorate every available space.{{cite web |url=https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/en/event/?mode=detail&id=4000000002698&isSpot=&isEvent=1|title=Summer Festival at Tanabata Shrine|publisher=[[Fukuoka Prefecture]] |accessdate=19 September 2019}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Tanabata-jinja
七夕神社 | image = Tanabata-jinja, Ogori, Fukuoka_03.jpg | caption = The ''[[honden]]'', or main shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|33|23|22.09|N|130|33|47.52|E|region:JP-40_type:landmark_source:kolossus-dewiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[The Cowherd and the Weaver Girl|Orihime]]
[[Himekoso-no-Kami]] | founded_by = | established = Unknown | date_destroyed = | location = 1-1, Ōzaki, [[Ogōri, Fukuoka|Ogōri]]
[[Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka]] 838-0127 | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Tanabata-jinja'''|七夕神社|}}, also known as Himekoso-jinja (媛社神社), is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ogōri, Fukuoka|Ogōri]], [[Fukuoka prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is dedicated to Orihime, the Japanese name of the Weaver Girl from the Chinese folk tale [[The Cowherd and the Weaver Girl|The Weaver Girl and the Cowherd]].{{cite web|url=https://goraifuku.jp/spots/detail/18|script-title=ja:媛社神社（七夕神社）|trans-title=Himekoso-jinja|accessdate=September 18, 2019|publisher=[[Fukuoka Prefecture]]|language=Japanese}} The weaver is celebrated in [[Tanabata]], a Japanese festival. ==History== According to the Hizen-Fudoki, an ancient record of the [[Hizen Province]], this shrine was extant in 730. The original object of worship at the shrine was a river, Yamaji-gawa, on the south of the shrine. In ''[[Engishiki]]'', a formal record on shrines written 1000 years ago, this area was developed by the textile industry. The villagers believe the weaving goddess known as ''Tanabata-tsume'' (棚機津女) from ancient times, and the story of the Cowherd and the Weaver Girl from [[China]] was assimilated, it is now known as the god of textiles. ==Festivals== Tanabata Grand festival, the annual summer festival held August 6-8 every year.{{cite web|url=http://ogorishinorekishi.ogori.net/小郡市の歴史遺産/七夕神社/|script-title=ja:小郡市の歴史遺産 七夕神社|trans-title=Tanabata-jinja, the historical site in Ogōri city|accessdate=September 18, 2019|publisher=[[ogori city]]|language=Japanese}} On the evening of August 6 children bring the ''[[mikoshi]]'' from the shrine, carry it around the neighborhoods that worship at the shrine. At 5 o'clock on the following day morning, the lion dance is performed as a prayer dedicated to the shrine. And later in the morning, the lion dancer and children pulling a float visit neighbors. In the evening, many open-air stalls line the path leading to the shrine, and wishes written on strips of paper sent from people throughout Japan decorate every available space.{{cite web |url=https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/en/event/?mode=detail&id=4000000002698&isSpot=&isEvent=1|title=Summer Festival at Tanabata Shrine|publisher=[[Fukuoka Prefecture]] |accessdate=19 September 2019}} ==Gallery== File:Tanabata-jinja, Ogori, Fukuoka 01.jpg|Torii at Tanabata Shrine entrance. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== It is dedicated to Orihime, the Japanese name of the Weaver Girl from the Chinese folk tale [[The Cowherd and the Weaver Girl|The Weaver Girl and the Cowherd]].{{cite web|url=https://goraifuku.jp/spots/detail/18|script-title=ja:媛社神社（七夕神社）|trans-title=Himekoso-jinja|accessdate=September 18, 2019|publisher=[[Fukuoka Prefecture]]|language=Japanese}} The weaver is celebrated in [[Tanabata]], a Japanese festival. ==History== According to the Hizen-Fudoki, an ancient record of the [[Hizen Province]], this shrine was extant in 730. In ''[[Engishiki]]'', a formal record on shrines written 1000 years ago, this area was developed by the textile industry. In the evening, many open-air stalls line the path leading to the shrine, and wishes written on strips of paper sent from people throughout Japan decorate every available space.{{cite web {{Infobox religious building | name = Tanabata-jinja
七夕神社 | image = Tanabata-jinja, Ogori, Fukuoka_03.jpg | caption = The ''[[honden]]'', or main shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|33|23|22.09|N|130|33|47.52|E|region:JP-40_type:landmark_source:kolossus-dewiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[The Cowherd and the Weaver Girl|Orihime]]
[[Himekoso-no-Kami]] | founded_by = | established = Unknown | date_destroyed = | location = 1-1, Ōzaki, [[Ogōri, Fukuoka|Ogōri]]
[[Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka]] 838-0127 | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Tanabata-jinja'''|七夕神社|}}, also known as Himekoso-jinja (媛社神社), is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ogōri, Fukuoka|Ogōri]], [[Fukuoka prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is dedicated to Orihime, the Japanese name of the Weaver Girl from the Chinese folk tale [[The Cowherd and the Weaver Girl|The Weaver Girl and the Cowherd]].{{cite web|url=https://goraifuku.jp/spots/detail/18|script-title=ja:媛社神社（七夕神社）|trans-title=Himekoso-jinja|accessdate=September 18, 2019|publisher=[[Fukuoka Prefecture]]|language=Japanese}} The weaver is celebrated in [[Tanabata]], a Japanese festival. ==History== According to the Hizen-Fudoki, an ancient record of the [[Hizen Province]], this shrine was extant in 730. The original object of worship at the shrine was a river, Yamaji-gawa, on the south of the shrine. In ''[[Engishiki]]'', a formal record on shrines written 1000 years ago, this area was developed by the textile industry. The villagers believe the weaving goddess known as ''Tanabata-tsume'' (棚機津女) from ancient times, and the story of the Cowherd and the Weaver Girl from [[China]] was assimilated, it is now known as the god of textiles. ==Festivals== Tanabata Grand festival, the annual summer festival held August 6-8 every year.{{cite web|url=http://ogorishinorekishi.ogori.net/小郡市の歴史遺産/七夕神社/|script-title=ja:小郡市の歴史遺産 七夕神社|trans-title=Tanabata-jinja, the historical site in Ogōri city|accessdate=September 18, 2019|publisher=[[ogori city]]|language=Japanese}} On the evening of August 6 children bring the ''[[mikoshi]]'' from the shrine, carry it around the neighborhoods that worship at the shrine. At 5 o'clock on the following day morning, the lion dance is performed as a prayer dedicated to the shrine. And later in the morning, the lion dancer and children pulling a float visit neighbors. In the evening, many open-air stalls line the path leading to the shrine, and wishes written on strips of paper sent from people throughout Japan decorate every available space.{{cite web |url=https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/en/event/?mode=detail&id=4000000002698&isSpot=&isEvent=1|title=Summer Festival at Tanabata Shrine|publisher=[[Fukuoka Prefecture]] |accessdate=19 September 2019}} ==Gallery== File:Tanabata-jinja, Ogori, Fukuoka 01.jpg|Torii at Tanabata Shrine entrance. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Infobox religious building | name = Tanabata-jinja
七夕神社 | image = Tanabata-jinja, Ogori, Fukuoka_03.jpg | caption = The ''[[honden]]'', or main shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|33|23|22.09|N|130|33|47.52|E|region:JP-40_type:landmark_source:kolossus-dewiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[The Cowherd and the Weaver Girl|Orihime]]
[[Himekoso-no-Kami]] | founded_by = | established = Unknown | date_destroyed = | location = 1-1, Ōzaki, [[Ogōri, Fukuoka|Ogōri]]
[[Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka]] 838-0127 | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Tanabata-jinja'''|七夕神社|}}, also known as Himekoso-jinja (媛社神社), is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ogōri, Fukuoka|Ogōri]], [[Fukuoka prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is dedicated to Orihime, the Japanese name of the Weaver Girl from the Chinese folk tale [[The Cowherd and the Weaver Girl|The Weaver Girl and the Cowherd]].{{cite web|url=https://goraifuku.jp/spots/detail/18|script-title=ja:媛社神社（七夕神社）|trans-title=Himekoso-jinja|accessdate=September 18, 2019|publisher=[[Fukuoka Prefecture]]|language=Japanese}} The weaver is celebrated in [[Tanabata]], a Japanese festival. ==History== According to the Hizen-Fudoki, an ancient record of the [[Hizen Province]], this shrine was extant in 730. The original object of worship at the shrine was a river, Yamaji-gawa, on the south of the shrine. In ''[[Engishiki]]'', a formal record on shrines written 1000 years ago, this area was developed by the textile industry. The villagers believe the weaving goddess known as ''Tanabata-tsume'' (棚機津女) from ancient times, and the story of the Cowherd and the Weaver Girl from [[China]] was assimilated, it is now known as the god of textiles. ==Festivals== Tanabata Grand festival, the annual summer festival held August 6-8 every year.{{cite web|url=http://ogorishinorekishi.ogori.net/小郡市の歴史遺産/七夕神社/|script-title=ja:小郡市の歴史遺産 七夕神社|trans-title=Tanabata-jinja, the historical site in Ogōri city|accessdate=September 18, 2019|publisher=[[ogori city]]|language=Japanese}} On the evening of August 6 children bring the ''[[mikoshi]]'' from the shrine, carry it around the neighborhoods that worship at the shrine. At 5 o'clock on the following day morning, the lion dance is performed as a prayer dedicated to the shrine. And later in the morning, the lion dancer and children pulling a float visit neighbors. In the evening, many open-air stalls line the path leading to the shrine, and wishes written on strips of paper sent from people throughout Japan decorate every available space.{{cite web |url=https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/en/event/?mode=detail&id=4000000002698&isSpot=&isEvent=1|title=Summer Festival at Tanabata Shrine|publisher=[[Fukuoka Prefecture]] |accessdate=19 September 2019}} ==Gallery== File:Tanabata-jinja, Ogori, Fukuoka 01.jpg|Torii at Tanabata Shrine entrance. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== It is dedicated to Orihime, the Japanese name of the Weaver Girl from the Chinese folk tale [[The Cowherd and the Weaver Girl|The Weaver Girl and the Cowherd]].{{cite web|url=https://goraifuku.jp/spots/detail/18|script-title=ja:媛社神社（七夕神社）|trans-title=Himekoso-jinja|accessdate=September 18, 2019|publisher=[[Fukuoka Prefecture]]|language=Japanese}} The weaver is celebrated in [[Tanabata]], a Japanese festival. ==History== According to the Hizen-Fudoki, an ancient record of the [[Hizen Province]], this shrine was extant in 730. The original object of worship at the shrine was a river, Yamaji-gawa, on the south of the shrine. In ''[[Engishiki]]'', a formal record on shrines written 1000 years ago, this area was developed by the textile industry. The villagers believe the weaving goddess known as ''Tanabata-tsume'' (棚機津女) from ancient times, and the story of the Cowherd and the Weaver Girl from [[China]] was assimilated, it is now known as the god of textiles. ==Festivals== Tanabata Grand festival, the annual summer festival held August 6-8 every year.{{cite web|url=http://ogorishinorekishi.ogori.net/小郡市の歴史遺産/七夕神社/|script-title=ja:小郡市の歴史遺産 七夕神社|trans-title=Tanabata-jinja, the historical site in Ogōri city|accessdate=September 18, 2019|publisher=[[ogori city]]|language=Japanese}} On the evening of August 6 children bring the ''[[mikoshi]]'' from the shrine, carry it around the neighborhoods that worship at the shrine. At 5 o'clock on the following day morning, the lion dance is performed as a prayer dedicated to the shrine. And later in the morning, the lion dancer and children pulling a float visit neighbors. In the evening, many open-air stalls line the path leading to the shrine, and wishes written on strips of paper sent from people throughout Japan decorate every available space.{{cite web |url=https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/en/event/?mode=detail&id=4000000002698&isSpot=&isEvent=1|title=Summer Festival at Tanabata Shrine|publisher=[[Fukuoka Prefecture]] |accessdate=19 September 2019}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Tanabata-jinja
七夕神社 | image = Tanabata-jinja, Ogori, Fukuoka_03.jpg | caption = The ''[[honden]]'', or main shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|33|23|22.09|N|130|33|47.52|E|region:JP-40_type:landmark_source:kolossus-dewiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[The Cowherd and the Weaver Girl|Orihime]]
[[Himekoso-no-Kami]] | founded_by = | established = Unknown | date_destroyed = | location = 1-1, Ōzaki, [[Ogōri, Fukuoka|Ogōri]]
[[Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka]] 838-0127 | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Tanabata-jinja'''|七夕神社|}}, also known as Himekoso-jinja (媛社神社), is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ogōri, Fukuoka|Ogōri]], [[Fukuoka prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is dedicated to Orihime, the Japanese name of the Weaver Girl from the Chinese folk tale [[The Cowherd and the Weaver Girl|The Weaver Girl and the Cowherd]].{{cite web|url=https://goraifuku.jp/spots/detail/18|script-title=ja:媛社神社（七夕神社）|trans-title=Himekoso-jinja|accessdate=September 18, 2019|publisher=[[Fukuoka Prefecture]]|language=Japanese}} The weaver is celebrated in [[Tanabata]], a Japanese festival. ==History== According to the Hizen-Fudoki, an ancient record of the [[Hizen Province]], this shrine was extant in 730. The original object of worship at the shrine was a river, Yamaji-gawa, on the south of the shrine. In ''[[Engishiki]]'', a formal record on shrines written 1000 years ago, this area was developed by the textile industry. The villagers believe the weaving goddess known as ''Tanabata-tsume'' (棚機津女) from ancient times, and the story of the Cowherd and the Weaver Girl from [[China]] was assimilated, it is now known as the god of textiles. ==Festivals== Tanabata Grand festival, the annual summer festival held August 6-8 every year.{{cite web|url=http://ogorishinorekishi.ogori.net/小郡市の歴史遺産/七夕神社/|script-title=ja:小郡市の歴史遺産 七夕神社|trans-title=Tanabata-jinja, the historical site in Ogōri city|accessdate=September 18, 2019|publisher=[[ogori city]]|language=Japanese}} On the evening of August 6 children bring the ''[[mikoshi]]'' from the shrine, carry it around the neighborhoods that worship at the shrine. At 5 o'clock on the following day morning, the lion dance is performed as a prayer dedicated to the shrine. And later in the morning, the lion dancer and children pulling a float visit neighbors. In the evening, many open-air stalls line the path leading to the shrine, and wishes written on strips of paper sent from people throughout Japan decorate every available space.{{cite web |url=https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/en/event/?mode=detail&id=4000000002698&isSpot=&isEvent=1|title=Summer Festival at Tanabata Shrine|publisher=[[Fukuoka Prefecture]] |accessdate=19 September 2019}} ==Gallery== File:Tanabata-jinja, Ogori, Fukuoka 01.jpg|Torii at Tanabata Shrine entrance. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== 七夕神社 | image = Tanabata-jinja, Ogori, Fukuoka_03.jpg | caption = The ''[[honden]]'', or main shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|33|23|22.09|N|130|33|47.52|E|region:JP-40_type:landmark_source:kolossus-dewiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[The Cowherd and the Weaver Girl|Orihime]]
[[Himekoso-no-Kami]] | founded_by = | established = Unknown | date_destroyed = | location = 1-1, Ōzaki, [[Ogōri, Fukuoka|Ogōri]]
[[Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka]] 838-0127 | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Tanabata-jinja'''|七夕神社|}}, also known as Himekoso-jinja (媛社神社), is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ogōri, Fukuoka|Ogōri]], [[Fukuoka prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is dedicated to Orihime, the Japanese name of the Weaver Girl from the Chinese folk tale [[The Cowherd and the Weaver Girl|The Weaver Girl and the Cowherd]].{{cite web|url=https://goraifuku.jp/spots/detail/18|script-title=ja:媛社神社（七夕神社）|trans-title=Himekoso-jinja|accessdate=September 18, 2019|publisher=[[Fukuoka Prefecture]]|language=Japanese}} The weaver is celebrated in [[Tanabata]], a Japanese festival. ==History== According to the Hizen-Fudoki, an ancient record of the [[Hizen Province]], this shrine was extant in 730. The original object of worship at the shrine was a river, Yamaji-gawa, on the south of the shrine. In ''[[Engishiki]]'', a formal record on shrines written 1000 years ago, this area was developed by the textile industry. The villagers believe the weaving goddess known as ''Tanabata-tsume'' (棚機津女) from ancient times, and the story of the Cowherd and the Weaver Girl from [[China]] was assimilated, it is now known as the god of textiles. ==Festivals== Tanabata Grand festival, the annual summer festival held August 6-8 every year.{{cite web|url=http://ogorishinorekishi.ogori.net/小郡市の歴史遺産/七夕神社/|script-title=ja:小郡市の歴史遺産 七夕神社|trans-title=Tanabata-jinja, the historical site in Ogōri city|accessdate=September 18, 2019|publisher=[[ogori city]]|language=Japanese}} On the evening of August 6 children bring the ''[[mikoshi]]'' from the shrine, carry it around the neighborhoods that worship at the shrine. At 5 o'clock on the following day morning, the lion dance is performed as a prayer dedicated to the shrine. And later in the morning, the lion dancer and children pulling a float visit neighbors. In the evening, many open-air stalls line the path leading to the shrine, and wishes written on strips of paper sent from people throughout Japan decorate every available space.{{cite web |url=https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/en/event/?mode=detail&id=4000000002698&isSpot=&isEvent=1|title=Summer Festival at Tanabata Shrine|publisher=[[Fukuoka Prefecture]] |accessdate=19 September 2019}} ==Gallery== File:Tanabata-jinja, Ogori, Fukuoka 01.jpg|Torii at Tanabata Shrine entrance. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== [[Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka]] 838-0127 | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Tanabata-jinja'''|七夕神社|}}, also known as Himekoso-jinja (媛社神社), is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ogōri, Fukuoka|Ogōri]], [[Fukuoka prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is dedicated to Orihime, the Japanese name of the Weaver Girl from the Chinese folk tale [[The Cowherd and the Weaver Girl|The Weaver Girl and the Cowherd]].{{cite web|url=https://goraifuku.jp/spots/detail/18|script-title=ja:媛社神社（七夕神社）|trans-title=Himekoso-jinja|accessdate=September 18, 2019|publisher=[[Fukuoka Prefecture]]|language=Japanese}} The weaver is celebrated in [[Tanabata]], a Japanese festival. ==History== According to the Hizen-Fudoki, an ancient record of the [[Hizen Province]], this shrine was extant in 730. The original object of worship at the shrine was a river, Yamaji-gawa, on the south of the shrine. In ''[[Engishiki]]'', a formal record on shrines written 1000 years ago, this area was developed by the textile industry. The villagers believe the weaving goddess known as ''Tanabata-tsume'' (棚機津女) from ancient times, and the story of the Cowherd and the Weaver Girl from [[China]] was assimilated, it is now known as the god of textiles. ==Festivals== Tanabata Grand festival, the annual summer festival held August 6-8 every year.{{cite web|url=http://ogorishinorekishi.ogori.net/小郡市の歴史遺産/七夕神社/|script-title=ja:小郡市の歴史遺産 七夕神社|trans-title=Tanabata-jinja, the historical site in Ogōri city|accessdate=September 18, 2019|publisher=[[ogori city]]|language=Japanese}} On the evening of August 6 children bring the ''[[mikoshi]]'' from the shrine, carry it around the neighborhoods that worship at the shrine. At 5 o'clock on the following day morning, the lion dance is performed as a prayer dedicated to the shrine. And later in the morning, the lion dancer and children pulling a float visit neighbors. In the evening, many open-air stalls line the path leading to the shrine, and wishes written on strips of paper sent from people throughout Japan decorate every available space.{{cite web |url=https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/en/event/?mode=detail&id=4000000002698&isSpot=&isEvent=1|title=Summer Festival at Tanabata Shrine|publisher=[[Fukuoka Prefecture]] |accessdate=19 September 2019}} ==Gallery== File:Tanabata-jinja, Ogori, Fukuoka 01.jpg|Torii at Tanabata Shrine entrance. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== [[Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka]] 838-0127 | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Tanabata-jinja'''|七夕神社|}}, also known as Himekoso-jinja (媛社神社), is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ogōri, Fukuoka|Ogōri]], [[Fukuoka prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is dedicated to Orihime, the Japanese name of the Weaver Girl from the Chinese folk tale [[The Cowherd and the Weaver Girl|The Weaver Girl and the Cowherd]].{{cite web|url=https://goraifuku.jp/spots/detail/18|script-title=ja:媛社神社（七夕神社）|trans-title=Himekoso-jinja|accessdate=September 18, 2019|publisher=[[Fukuoka Prefecture]]|language=Japanese}} The weaver is celebrated in [[Tanabata]], a Japanese festival. ==History== According to the Hizen-Fudoki, an ancient record of the [[Hizen Province]], this shrine was extant in 730. The original object of worship at the shrine was a river, Yamaji-gawa, on the south of the shrine. In ''[[Engishiki]]'', a formal record on shrines written 1000 years ago, this area was developed by the textile industry. The villagers believe the weaving goddess known as ''Tanabata-tsume'' (棚機津女) from ancient times, and the story of the Cowherd and the Weaver Girl from [[China]] was assimilated, it is now known as the god of textiles. ==Festivals== Tanabata Grand festival, the annual summer festival held August 6-8 every year.{{cite web|url=http://ogorishinorekishi.ogori.net/小郡市の歴史遺産/七夕神社/|script-title=ja:小郡市の歴史遺産 七夕神社|trans-title=Tanabata-jinja, the historical site in Ogōri city|accessdate=September 18, 2019|publisher=[[ogori city]]|language=Japanese}} On the evening of August 6 children bring the ''[[mikoshi]]'' from the shrine, carry it around the neighborhoods that worship at the shrine. At 5 o'clock on the following day morning, the lion dance is performed as a prayer dedicated to the shrine. And later in the morning, the lion dancer and children pulling a float visit neighbors. In the evening, many open-air stalls line the path leading to the shrine, and wishes written on strips of paper sent from people throughout Japan decorate every available space.{{cite web |url=https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/en/event/?mode=detail&id=4000000002698&isSpot=&isEvent=1|title=Summer Festival at Tanabata Shrine|publisher=[[Fukuoka Prefecture]] |accessdate=19 September 2019}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Tanabata-jinja
七夕神社 | image = Tanabata-jinja, Ogori, Fukuoka_03.jpg | caption = The ''[[honden]]'', or main shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|33|23|22.09|N|130|33|47.52|E|region:JP-40_type:landmark_source:kolossus-dewiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[The Cowherd and the Weaver Girl|Orihime]]
[[Himekoso-no-Kami]] | founded_by = | established = Unknown | date_destroyed = | location = 1-1, Ōzaki, [[Ogōri, Fukuoka|Ogōri]]
[[Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka]] 838-0127 | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Tanabata-jinja'''|七夕神社|}}, also known as Himekoso-jinja (媛社神社), is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ogōri, Fukuoka|Ogōri]], [[Fukuoka prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is dedicated to Orihime, the Japanese name of the Weaver Girl from the Chinese folk tale [[The Cowherd and the Weaver Girl|The Weaver Girl and the Cowherd]].{{cite web|url=https://goraifuku.jp/spots/detail/18|script-title=ja:媛社神社（七夕神社）|trans-title=Himekoso-jinja|accessdate=September 18, 2019|publisher=[[Fukuoka Prefecture]]|language=Japanese}} The weaver is celebrated in [[Tanabata]], a Japanese festival. ==History== According to the Hizen-Fudoki, an ancient record of the [[Hizen Province]], this shrine was extant in 730. The original object of worship at the shrine was a river, Yamaji-gawa, on the south of the shrine. In ''[[Engishiki]]'', a formal record on shrines written 1000 years ago, this area was developed by the textile industry. The villagers believe the weaving goddess known as ''Tanabata-tsume'' (棚機津女) from ancient times, and the story of the Cowherd and the Weaver Girl from [[China]] was assimilated, it is now known as the god of textiles. ==Festivals== Tanabata Grand festival, the annual summer festival held August 6-8 every year.{{cite web|url=http://ogorishinorekishi.ogori.net/小郡市の歴史遺産/七夕神社/|script-title=ja:小郡市の歴史遺産 七夕神社|trans-title=Tanabata-jinja, the historical site in Ogōri city|accessdate=September 18, 2019|publisher=[[ogori city]]|language=Japanese}} On the evening of August 6 children bring the ''[[mikoshi]]'' from the shrine, carry it around the neighborhoods that worship at the shrine. At 5 o'clock on the following day morning, the lion dance is performed as a prayer dedicated to the shrine. And later in the morning, the lion dancer and children pulling a float visit neighbors. In the evening, many open-air stalls line the path leading to the shrine, and wishes written on strips of paper sent from people throughout Japan decorate every available space.{{cite web |url=https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/en/event/?mode=detail&id=4000000002698&isSpot=&isEvent=1|title=Summer Festival at Tanabata Shrine|publisher=[[Fukuoka Prefecture]] |accessdate=19 September 2019}} It is dedicated to Orihime, the Japanese name of the Weaver Girl from the Chinese folk tale [[The Cowherd and the Weaver Girl|The Weaver Girl and the Cowherd]].{{cite web|url=https://goraifuku.jp/spots/detail/18|script-title=ja:媛社神社（七夕神社）|trans-title=Himekoso-jinja|accessdate=September 18, 2019|publisher=[[Fukuoka Prefecture]]|language=Japanese}} The weaver is celebrated in [[Tanabata]], a Japanese festival. ==History== According to the Hizen-Fudoki, an ancient record of the [[Hizen Province]], this shrine was extant in 730. The original object of worship at the shrine was a river, Yamaji-gawa, on the south of the shrine. In ''[[Engishiki]]'', a formal record on shrines written 1000 years ago, this area was developed by the textile industry. The villagers believe the weaving goddess known as ''Tanabata-tsume'' (棚機津女) from ancient times, and the story of the Cowherd and the Weaver Girl from [[China]] was assimilated, it is now known as the god of textiles. ==Festivals== Tanabata Grand festival, the annual summer festival held August 6-8 every year.{{cite web|url=http://ogorishinorekishi.ogori.net/小郡市の歴史遺産/七夕神社/|script-title=ja:小郡市の歴史遺産 七夕神社|trans-title=Tanabata-jinja, the historical site in Ogōri city|accessdate=September 18, 2019|publisher=[[ogori city]]|language=Japanese}} On the evening of August 6 children bring the ''[[mikoshi]]'' from the shrine, carry it around the neighborhoods that worship at the shrine. At 5 o'clock on the following day morning, the lion dance is performed as a prayer dedicated to the shrine. And later in the morning, the lion dancer and children pulling a float visit neighbors. In the evening, many open-air stalls line the path leading to the shrine, and wishes written on strips of paper sent from people throughout Japan decorate every available space.{{cite web |url=https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/en/event/?mode=detail&id=4000000002698&isSpot=&isEvent=1|title=Summer Festival at Tanabata Shrine|publisher=[[Fukuoka Prefecture]] |accessdate=19 September 2019}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Tanabata-jinja
七夕神社 | image = Tanabata-jinja, Ogori, Fukuoka_03.jpg | caption = The ''[[honden]]'', or main shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|33|23|22.09|N|130|33|47.52|E|region:JP-40_type:landmark_source:kolossus-dewiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[The Cowherd and the Weaver Girl|Orihime]]
[[Himekoso-no-Kami]] | founded_by = | established = Unknown | date_destroyed = | location = 1-1, Ōzaki, [[Ogōri, Fukuoka|Ogōri]]
[[Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka]] 838-0127 | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Tanabata-jinja'''|七夕神社|}}, also known as Himekoso-jinja (媛社神社), is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ogōri, Fukuoka|Ogōri]], [[Fukuoka prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is dedicated to Orihime, the Japanese name of the Weaver Girl from the Chinese folk tale [[The Cowherd and the Weaver Girl|The Weaver Girl and the Cowherd]].{{cite web|url=https://goraifuku.jp/spots/detail/18|script-title=ja:媛社神社（七夕神社）|trans-title=Himekoso-jinja|accessdate=September 18, 2019|publisher=[[Fukuoka Prefecture]]|language=Japanese}} The weaver is celebrated in [[Tanabata]], a Japanese festival. ==History== According to the Hizen-Fudoki, an ancient record of the [[Hizen Province]], this shrine was extant in 730. The original object of worship at the shrine was a river, Yamaji-gawa, on the south of the shrine. In ''[[Engishiki]]'', a formal record on shrines written 1000 years ago, this area was developed by the textile industry. The villagers believe the weaving goddess known as ''Tanabata-tsume'' (棚機津女) from ancient times, and the story of the Cowherd and the Weaver Girl from [[China]] was assimilated, it is now known as the god of textiles. ==Festivals== Tanabata Grand festival, the annual summer festival held August 6-8 every year.{{cite web|url=http://ogorishinorekishi.ogori.net/小郡市の歴史遺産/七夕神社/|script-title=ja:小郡市の歴史遺産 七夕神社|trans-title=Tanabata-jinja, the historical site in Ogōri city|accessdate=September 18, 2019|publisher=[[ogori city]]|language=Japanese}} On the evening of August 6 children bring the ''[[mikoshi]]'' from the shrine, carry it around the neighborhoods that worship at the shrine. At 5 o'clock on the following day morning, the lion dance is performed as a prayer dedicated to the shrine. And later in the morning, the lion dancer and children pulling a float visit neighbors. In the evening, many open-air stalls line the path leading to the shrine, and wishes written on strips of paper sent from people throughout Japan decorate every available space.{{cite web |url=https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/en/event/?mode=detail&id=4000000002698&isSpot=&isEvent=1|title=Summer Festival at Tanabata Shrine|publisher=[[Fukuoka Prefecture]] |accessdate=19 September 2019}} ==Gallery== File:Tanabata-jinja, Ogori, Fukuoka 01.jpg|Torii at Tanabata Shrine entrance. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== It is dedicated to Orihime, the Japanese name of the Weaver Girl from the Chinese folk tale [[The Cowherd and the Weaver Girl|The Weaver Girl and the Cowherd]].{{cite web|url=https://goraifuku.jp/spots/detail/18|script-title=ja:媛社神社（七夕神社）|trans-title=Himekoso-jinja|accessdate=September 18, 2019|publisher=[[Fukuoka Prefecture]]|language=Japanese}} The weaver is celebrated in [[Tanabata]], a Japanese festival. ==History== According to the Hizen-Fudoki, an ancient record of the [[Hizen Province]], this shrine was extant in 730. The villagers believe the weaving goddess known as ''Tanabata-tsume'' (棚機津女) from ancient times, and the story of the Cowherd and the Weaver Girl from [[China]] was assimilated, it is now known as the god of textiles. ==Festivals== Tanabata Grand festival, the annual summer festival held August 6-8 every year.{{cite web|url=http://ogorishinorekishi.ogori.net/小郡市の歴史遺産/七夕神社/|script-title=ja:小郡市の歴史遺産 七夕神社|trans-title=Tanabata-jinja, the historical site in Ogōri city|accessdate=September 18, 2019|publisher=[[ogori city]]|language=Japanese}} On the evening of August 6 children bring the ''[[mikoshi]]'' from the shrine, carry it around the neighborhoods that worship at the shrine. In the evening, many open-air stalls line the path leading to the shrine, and wishes written on strips of paper sent from people throughout Japan decorate every available space.{{cite web |url=https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/en/event/?mode=detail&id=4000000002698&isSpot=&isEvent=1|title=Summer Festival at Tanabata Shrine|publisher=[[Fukuoka Prefecture]] |accessdate=19 September 2019}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Tanabata-jinja
七夕神社 | image = Tanabata-jinja, Ogori, Fukuoka_03.jpg | caption = The ''[[honden]]'', or main shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|33|23|22.09|N|130|33|47.52|E|region:JP-40_type:landmark_source:kolossus-dewiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[The Cowherd and the Weaver Girl|Orihime]]
[[Himekoso-no-Kami]] | founded_by = | established = Unknown | date_destroyed = | location = 1-1, Ōzaki, [[Ogōri, Fukuoka|Ogōri]]
[[Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka]] 838-0127 | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Tanabata-jinja'''|七夕神社|}}, also known as Himekoso-jinja (媛社神社), is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ogōri, Fukuoka|Ogōri]], [[Fukuoka prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is dedicated to Orihime, the Japanese name of the Weaver Girl from the Chinese folk tale [[The Cowherd and the Weaver Girl|The Weaver Girl and the Cowherd]].{{cite web|url=https://goraifuku.jp/spots/detail/18|script-title=ja:媛社神社（七夕神社）|trans-title=Himekoso-jinja|accessdate=September 18, 2019|publisher=[[Fukuoka Prefecture]]|language=Japanese}} The weaver is celebrated in [[Tanabata]], a Japanese festival. ==History== According to the Hizen-Fudoki, an ancient record of the [[Hizen Province]], this shrine was extant in 730. The original object of worship at the shrine was a river, Yamaji-gawa, on the south of the shrine. In ''[[Engishiki]]'', a formal record on shrines written 1000 years ago, this area was developed by the textile industry. The villagers believe the weaving goddess known as ''Tanabata-tsume'' (棚機津女) from ancient times, and the story of the Cowherd and the Weaver Girl from [[China]] was assimilated, it is now known as the god of textiles. ==Festivals== Tanabata Grand festival, the annual summer festival held August 6-8 every year.{{cite web|url=http://ogorishinorekishi.ogori.net/小郡市の歴史遺産/七夕神社/|script-title=ja:小郡市の歴史遺産 七夕神社|trans-title=Tanabata-jinja, the historical site in Ogōri city|accessdate=September 18, 2019|publisher=[[ogori city]]|language=Japanese}} On the evening of August 6 children bring the ''[[mikoshi]]'' from the shrine, carry it around the neighborhoods that worship at the shrine. At 5 o'clock on the following day morning, the lion dance is performed as a prayer dedicated to the shrine. And later in the morning, the lion dancer and children pulling a float visit neighbors. In the evening, many open-air stalls line the path leading to the shrine, and wishes written on strips of paper sent from people throughout Japan decorate every available space.{{cite web |url=https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/en/event/?mode=detail&id=4000000002698&isSpot=&isEvent=1|title=Summer Festival at Tanabata Shrine|publisher=[[Fukuoka Prefecture]] |accessdate=19 September 2019}} ==Gallery== File:Tanabata-jinja, Ogori, Fukuoka 01.jpg|Torii at Tanabata Shrine entrance. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== The villagers believe the weaving goddess known as ''Tanabata-tsume'' (棚機津女) from ancient times, and the story of the Cowherd and the Weaver Girl from [[China]] was assimilated, it is now known as the god of textiles. ==Festivals== Tanabata Grand festival, the annual summer festival held August 6-8 every year.{{cite web|url=http://ogorishinorekishi.ogori.net/小郡市の歴史遺産/七夕神社/|script-title=ja:小郡市の歴史遺産 七夕神社|trans-title=Tanabata-jinja, the historical site in Ogōri city|accessdate=September 18, 2019|publisher=[[ogori city]]|language=Japanese}} On the evening of August 6 children bring the ''[[mikoshi]]'' from the shrine, carry it around the neighborhoods that worship at the shrine. At 5 o'clock on the following day morning, the lion dance is performed as a prayer dedicated to the shrine. And later in the morning, the lion dancer and children pulling a float visit neighbors. In the evening, many open-air stalls line the path leading to the shrine, and wishes written on strips of paper sent from people throughout Japan decorate every available space.{{cite web |url=https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/en/event/?mode=detail&id=4000000002698&isSpot=&isEvent=1|title=Summer Festival at Tanabata Shrine|publisher=[[Fukuoka Prefecture]] |accessdate=19 September 2019}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Tanabata-jinja
七夕神社 | image = Tanabata-jinja, Ogori, Fukuoka_03.jpg | caption = The ''[[honden]]'', or main shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|33|23|22.09|N|130|33|47.52|E|region:JP-40_type:landmark_source:kolossus-dewiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[The Cowherd and the Weaver Girl|Orihime]]
[[Himekoso-no-Kami]] | founded_by = | established = Unknown | date_destroyed = | location = 1-1, Ōzaki, [[Ogōri, Fukuoka|Ogōri]]
[[Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka]] 838-0127 | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Tanabata-jinja'''|七夕神社|}}, also known as Himekoso-jinja (媛社神社), is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ogōri, Fukuoka|Ogōri]], [[Fukuoka prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is dedicated to Orihime, the Japanese name of the Weaver Girl from the Chinese folk tale [[The Cowherd and the Weaver Girl|The Weaver Girl and the Cowherd]].{{cite web|url=https://goraifuku.jp/spots/detail/18|script-title=ja:媛社神社（七夕神社）|trans-title=Himekoso-jinja|accessdate=September 18, 2019|publisher=[[Fukuoka Prefecture]]|language=Japanese}} The weaver is celebrated in [[Tanabata]], a Japanese festival. ==History== According to the Hizen-Fudoki, an ancient record of the [[Hizen Province]], this shrine was extant in 730. The original object of worship at the shrine was a river, Yamaji-gawa, on the south of the shrine. In ''[[Engishiki]]'', a formal record on shrines written 1000 years ago, this area was developed by the textile industry. The villagers believe the weaving goddess known as ''Tanabata-tsume'' (棚機津女) from ancient times, and the story of the Cowherd and the Weaver Girl from [[China]] was assimilated, it is now known as the god of textiles. ==Festivals== Tanabata Grand festival, the annual summer festival held August 6-8 every year.{{cite web|url=http://ogorishinorekishi.ogori.net/小郡市の歴史遺産/七夕神社/|script-title=ja:小郡市の歴史遺産 七夕神社|trans-title=Tanabata-jinja, the historical site in Ogōri city|accessdate=September 18, 2019|publisher=[[ogori city]]|language=Japanese}} On the evening of August 6 children bring the ''[[mikoshi]]'' from the shrine, carry it around the neighborhoods that worship at the shrine. At 5 o'clock on the following day morning, the lion dance is performed as a prayer dedicated to the shrine. And later in the morning, the lion dancer and children pulling a float visit neighbors. In the evening, many open-air stalls line the path leading to the shrine, and wishes written on strips of paper sent from people throughout Japan decorate every available space.{{cite web |url=https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/en/event/?mode=detail&id=4000000002698&isSpot=&isEvent=1|title=Summer Festival at Tanabata Shrine|publisher=[[Fukuoka Prefecture]] |accessdate=19 September 2019}}
