{{Infobox religious building | name = Tanabata-jinja
七夕神社 | image = Tanabata-jinja, Ogori, Fukuoka_03.jpg | caption = The ''[[honden]]'', or main shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|33|23|22.09|N|130|33|47.52|E|region:JP-40_type:landmark_source:kolossus-dewiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[The Cowherd and the Weaver Girl|Orihime]]
[[Himekoso-no-Kami]] | founded_by = | established = Unknown | date_destroyed = | location = 1-1, Ōzaki, [[Ogōri, Fukuoka|Ogōri]]
[[Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka]] 838-0127 | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Tanabata-jinja'''|七夕神社|}}, also known as Himekoso-jinja (媛社神社), is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ogōri, Fukuoka|Ogōri]], [[Fukuoka prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is dedicated to Orihime, the Japanese name of the Weaver Girl from the Chinese folk tale [[The Cowherd and the Weaver Girl|The Weaver Girl and the Cowherd]].{{cite web|url=https://goraifuku.jp/spots/detail/18|script-title=ja:媛社神社（七夕神社）|trans-title=Himekoso-jinja|accessdate=September 18, 2019|publisher=[[Fukuoka Prefecture]]|language=Japanese}} The weaver is celebrated in [[Tanabata]], a Japanese festival. ==History== According to the Hizen-Fudoki, an ancient record of the [[Hizen Province]], this shrine was extant in 730. The original object of worship at the shrine was a river, Yamaji-gawa, on the south of the shrine. In ''[[Engishiki]]'', a formal record on shrines written 1000 years ago, this area was developed by the textile industry. The villagers believe the weaving goddess known as ''Tanabata-tsume'' (棚機津女) from ancient times, and the story of the Cowherd and the Weaver Girl from [[China]] was assimilated, it is now known as the god of textiles. ==Festivals== Tanabata Grand festival, the annual summer festival held August 6-8 every year.{{cite web|url=http://ogorishinorekishi.ogori.net/小郡市の歴史遺産/七夕神社/|script-title=ja:小郡市の歴史遺産 七夕神社|trans-title=Tanabata-jinja, the historical site in Ogōri city|accessdate=September 18, 2019|publisher=[[ogori city]]|language=Japanese}} On the evening of August 6 children bring the ''[[mikoshi]]'' from the shrine, carry it around the neighborhoods that worship at the shrine. At 5 o'clock on the following day morning, the lion dance is performed as a prayer dedicated to the shrine. And later in the morning, the lion dancer and children pulling a float visit neighbors. In the evening, many open-air stalls line the path leading to the shrine, and wishes written on strips of paper sent from people throughout Japan decorate every available space.{{cite web |url=https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/en/event/?mode=detail&id=4000000002698&isSpot=&isEvent=1|title=Summer Festival at Tanabata Shrine|publisher=[[Fukuoka Prefecture]] |accessdate=19 September 2019}} ==Gallery== File:Tanabata-jinja, Ogori, Fukuoka 01.jpg|Torii at Tanabata Shrine entrance. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Infobox religious building | name = Tanabata-jinja
七夕神社 | image = Tanabata-jinja, Ogori, Fukuoka_03.jpg | caption = The ''[[honden]]'', or main shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|33|23|22.09|N|130|33|47.52|E|region:JP-40_type:landmark_source:kolossus-dewiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[The Cowherd and the Weaver Girl|Orihime]]
[[Himekoso-no-Kami]] | founded_by = | established = Unknown | date_destroyed = | location = 1-1, Ōzaki, [[Ogōri, Fukuoka|Ogōri]]
[[Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka]] 838-0127 | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Tanabata-jinja'''|七夕神社|}}, also known as Himekoso-jinja (媛社神社), is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ogōri, Fukuoka|Ogōri]], [[Fukuoka prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is dedicated to Orihime, the Japanese name of the Weaver Girl from the Chinese folk tale [[The Cowherd and the Weaver Girl|The Weaver Girl and the Cowherd]].{{cite web|url=https://goraifuku.jp/spots/detail/18|script-title=ja:媛社神社（七夕神社）|trans-title=Himekoso-jinja|accessdate=September 18, 2019|publisher=[[Fukuoka Prefecture]]|language=Japanese}} The weaver is celebrated in [[Tanabata]], a Japanese festival. ==History== According to the Hizen-Fudoki, an ancient record of the [[Hizen Province]], this shrine was extant in 730. The original object of worship at the shrine was a river, Yamaji-gawa, on the south of the shrine. In ''[[Engishiki]]'', a formal record on shrines written 1000 years ago, this area was developed by the textile industry. The villagers believe the weaving goddess known as ''Tanabata-tsume'' (棚機津女) from ancient times, and the story of the Cowherd and the Weaver Girl from [[China]] was assimilated, it is now known as the god of textiles. ==Festivals== Tanabata Grand festival, the annual summer festival held August 6-8 every year.{{cite web|url=http://ogorishinorekishi.ogori.net/小郡市の歴史遺産/七夕神社/|script-title=ja:小郡市の歴史遺産 七夕神社|trans-title=Tanabata-jinja, the historical site in Ogōri city|accessdate=September 18, 2019|publisher=[[ogori city]]|language=Japanese}} On the evening of August 6 children bring the ''[[mikoshi]]'' from the shrine, carry it around the neighborhoods that worship at the shrine. At 5 o'clock on the following day morning, the lion dance is performed as a prayer dedicated to the shrine. And later in the morning, the lion dancer and children pulling a float visit neighbors. In the evening, many open-air stalls line the path leading to the shrine, and wishes written on strips of paper sent from people throughout Japan decorate every available space.{{cite web |url=https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/en/event/?mode=detail&id=4000000002698&isSpot=&isEvent=1|title=Summer Festival at Tanabata Shrine|publisher=[[Fukuoka Prefecture]] |accessdate=19 September 2019}} ==Gallery== File:Tanabata-jinja, Ogori, Fukuoka 01.jpg|Torii at Tanabata Shrine entrance. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building | name = Tanabata-jinja
七夕神社 | image = Tanabata-jinja, Ogori, Fukuoka_03.jpg | caption = The ''[[honden]]'', or main shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|33|23|22.09|N|130|33|47.52|E|region:JP-40_type:landmark_source:kolossus-dewiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[The Cowherd and the Weaver Girl|Orihime]]
[[Himekoso-no-Kami]] | founded_by = | established = Unknown | date_destroyed = | location = 1-1, Ōzaki, [[Ogōri, Fukuoka|Ogōri]]
[[Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka]] 838-0127 | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Tanabata-jinja'''|七夕神社|}}, also known as Himekoso-jinja (媛社神社), is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ogōri, Fukuoka|Ogōri]], [[Fukuoka prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is dedicated to Orihime, the Japanese name of the Weaver Girl from the Chinese folk tale [[The Cowherd and the Weaver Girl|The Weaver Girl and the Cowherd]].{{cite web|url=https://goraifuku.jp/spots/detail/18|script-title=ja:媛社神社（七夕神社）|trans-title=Himekoso-jinja|accessdate=September 18, 2019|publisher=[[Fukuoka Prefecture]]|language=Japanese}} The weaver is celebrated in [[Tanabata]], a Japanese festival. ==History== According to the Hizen-Fudoki, an ancient record of the [[Hizen Province]], this shrine was extant in 730. The original object of worship at the shrine was a river, Yamaji-gawa, on the south of the shrine. In ''[[Engishiki]]'', a formal record on shrines written 1000 years ago, this area was developed by the textile industry. The villagers believe the weaving goddess known as ''Tanabata-tsume'' (棚機津女) from ancient times, and the story of the Cowherd and the Weaver Girl from [[China]] was assimilated, it is now known as the god of textiles. ==Festivals== Tanabata Grand festival, the annual summer festival held August 6-8 every year.{{cite web|url=http://ogorishinorekishi.ogori.net/小郡市の歴史遺産/七夕神社/|script-title=ja:小郡市の歴史遺産 七夕神社|trans-title=Tanabata-jinja, the historical site in Ogōri city|accessdate=September 18, 2019|publisher=[[ogori city]]|language=Japanese}} On the evening of August 6 children bring the ''[[mikoshi]]'' from the shrine, carry it around the neighborhoods that worship at the shrine. At 5 o'clock on the following day morning, the lion dance is performed as a prayer dedicated to the shrine. And later in the morning, the lion dancer and children pulling a float visit neighbors. In the evening, many open-air stalls line the path leading to the shrine, and wishes written on strips of paper sent from people throughout Japan decorate every available space.{{cite web |url=https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/en/event/?mode=detail&id=4000000002698&isSpot=&isEvent=1|title=Summer Festival at Tanabata Shrine|publisher=[[Fukuoka Prefecture]] |accessdate=19 September 2019}} ==Gallery== File:Tanabata-jinja, Ogori, Fukuoka 01.jpg|Torii at Tanabata Shrine entrance. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== *[https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/en/event/?mode=detail&id=4000000000656 Tanabata Shrine - Fukuoka Prefecture Tourist Information] {{in lang|en}} {{Commons Category:Pages with redlinks to {{Infobox religious building | name = Tanabata-jinja
七夕神社 | image = Tanabata-jinja, Ogori, Fukuoka_03.jpg | caption = The ''[[honden]]'', or main shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|33|23|22.09|N|130|33|47.52|E|region:JP-40_type:landmark_source:kolossus-dewiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[The Cowherd and the Weaver Girl|Orihime]]
[[Himekoso-no-Kami]] | founded_by = | established = Unknown | date_destroyed = | location = 1-1, Ōzaki, [[Ogōri, Fukuoka|Ogōri]]
[[Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka]] 838-0127 | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Tanabata-jinja'''|七夕神社|}}, also known as Himekoso-jinja (媛社神社), is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ogōri, Fukuoka|Ogōri]], [[Fukuoka prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is dedicated to Orihime, the Japanese name of the Weaver Girl from the Chinese folk tale [[The Cowherd and the Weaver Girl|The Weaver Girl and the Cowherd]].{{cite web|url=https://goraifuku.jp/spots/detail/18|script-title=ja:媛社神社（七夕神社）|trans-title=Himekoso-jinja|accessdate=September 18, 2019|publisher=[[Fukuoka Prefecture]]|language=Japanese}} The weaver is celebrated in [[Tanabata]], a Japanese festival. ==History== According to the Hizen-Fudoki, an ancient record of the [[Hizen Province]], this shrine was extant in 730. The original object of worship at the shrine was a river, Yamaji-gawa, on the south of the shrine. In ''[[Engishiki]]'', a formal record on shrines written 1000 years ago, this area was developed by the textile industry. The villagers believe the weaving goddess known as ''Tanabata-tsume'' (棚機津女) from ancient times, and the story of the Cowherd and the Weaver Girl from [[China]] was assimilated, it is now known as the god of textiles. ==Festivals== Tanabata Grand festival, the annual summer festival held August 6-8 every year.{{cite web|url=http://ogorishinorekishi.ogori.net/小郡市の歴史遺産/七夕神社/|script-title=ja:小郡市の歴史遺産 七夕神社|trans-title=Tanabata-jinja, the historical site in Ogōri city|accessdate=September 18, 2019|publisher=[[ogori city]]|language=Japanese}} On the evening of August 6 children bring the ''[[mikoshi]]'' from the shrine, carry it around the neighborhoods that worship at the shrine. At 5 o'clock on the following day morning, the lion dance is performed as a prayer dedicated to the shrine. And later in the morning, the lion dancer and children pulling a float visit neighbors. In the evening, many open-air stalls line the path leading to the shrine, and wishes written on strips of paper sent from people throughout Japan decorate every available space.{{cite web |url=https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/en/event/?mode=detail&id=4000000002698&isSpot=&isEvent=1|title=Summer Festival at Tanabata Shrine|publisher=[[Fukuoka Prefecture]] |accessdate=19 September 2019}} [[Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka]] 838-0127 | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Tanabata-jinja'''|七夕神社|}}, also known as Himekoso-jinja (媛社神社), is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ogōri, Fukuoka|Ogōri]], [[Fukuoka prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is dedicated to Orihime, the Japanese name of the Weaver Girl from the Chinese folk tale [[The Cowherd and the Weaver Girl|The Weaver Girl and the Cowherd]].{{cite web|url=https://goraifuku.jp/spots/detail/18|script-title=ja:媛社神社（七夕神社）|trans-title=Himekoso-jinja|accessdate=September 18, 2019|publisher=[[Fukuoka Prefecture]]|language=Japanese}} The weaver is celebrated in [[Tanabata]], a Japanese festival. ==History== According to the Hizen-Fudoki, an ancient record of the [[Hizen Province]], this shrine was extant in 730. The original object of worship at the shrine was a river, Yamaji-gawa, on the south of the shrine. In ''[[Engishiki]]'', a formal record on shrines written 1000 years ago, this area was developed by the textile industry. The villagers believe the weaving goddess known as ''Tanabata-tsume'' (棚機津女) from ancient times, and the story of the Cowherd and the Weaver Girl from [[China]] was assimilated, it is now known as the god of textiles. ==Festivals== Tanabata Grand festival, the annual summer festival held August 6-8 every year.{{cite web|url=http://ogorishinorekishi.ogori.net/小郡市の歴史遺産/七夕神社/|script-title=ja:小郡市の歴史遺産 七夕神社|trans-title=Tanabata-jinja, the historical site in Ogōri city|accessdate=September 18, 2019|publisher=[[ogori city]]|language=Japanese}} On the evening of August 6 children bring the ''[[mikoshi]]'' from the shrine, carry it around the neighborhoods that worship at the shrine. At 5 o'clock on the following day morning, the lion dance is performed as a prayer dedicated to the shrine. And later in the morning, the lion dancer and children pulling a float visit neighbors. In the evening, many open-air stalls line the path leading to the shrine, and wishes written on strips of paper sent from people throughout Japan decorate every available space.{{cite web |url=https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/en/event/?mode=detail&id=4000000002698&isSpot=&isEvent=1|title=Summer Festival at Tanabata Shrine|publisher=[[Fukuoka Prefecture]] |accessdate=19 September 2019}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Tanabata-jinja
七夕神社 | image = Tanabata-jinja, Ogori, Fukuoka_03.jpg | caption = The ''[[honden]]'', or main shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|33|23|22.09|N|130|33|47.52|E|region:JP-40_type:landmark_source:kolossus-dewiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[The Cowherd and the Weaver Girl|Orihime]]
[[Himekoso-no-Kami]] | founded_by = | established = Unknown | date_destroyed = | location = 1-1, Ōzaki, [[Ogōri, Fukuoka|Ogōri]]
[[Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka]] 838-0127 | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Tanabata-jinja'''|七夕神社|}}, also known as Himekoso-jinja (媛社神社), is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ogōri, Fukuoka|Ogōri]], [[Fukuoka prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is dedicated to Orihime, the Japanese name of the Weaver Girl from the Chinese folk tale [[The Cowherd and the Weaver Girl|The Weaver Girl and the Cowherd]].{{cite web|url=https://goraifuku.jp/spots/detail/18|script-title=ja:媛社神社（七夕神社）|trans-title=Himekoso-jinja|accessdate=September 18, 2019|publisher=[[Fukuoka Prefecture]]|language=Japanese}} The weaver is celebrated in [[Tanabata]], a Japanese festival. ==History== According to the Hizen-Fudoki, an ancient record of the [[Hizen Province]], this shrine was extant in 730. The original object of worship at the shrine was a river, Yamaji-gawa, on the south of the shrine. In ''[[Engishiki]]'', a formal record on shrines written 1000 years ago, this area was developed by the textile industry. The villagers believe the weaving goddess known as ''Tanabata-tsume'' (棚機津女) from ancient times, and the story of the Cowherd and the Weaver Girl from [[China]] was assimilated, it is now known as the god of textiles. ==Festivals== Tanabata Grand festival, the annual summer festival held August 6-8 every year.{{cite web|url=http://ogorishinorekishi.ogori.net/小郡市の歴史遺産/七夕神社/|script-title=ja:小郡市の歴史遺産 七夕神社|trans-title=Tanabata-jinja, the historical site in Ogōri city|accessdate=September 18, 2019|publisher=[[ogori city]]|language=Japanese}} On the evening of August 6 children bring the ''[[mikoshi]]'' from the shrine, carry it around the neighborhoods that worship at the shrine. At 5 o'clock on the following day morning, the lion dance is performed as a prayer dedicated to the shrine. And later in the morning, the lion dancer and children pulling a float visit neighbors. In the evening, many open-air stalls line the path leading to the shrine, and wishes written on strips of paper sent from people throughout Japan decorate every available space.{{cite web |url=https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/en/event/?mode=detail&id=4000000002698&isSpot=&isEvent=1|title=Summer Festival at Tanabata Shrine|publisher=[[Fukuoka Prefecture]] |accessdate=19 September 2019}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Tanabata-jinja
七夕神社 | image = Tanabata-jinja, Ogori, Fukuoka_03.jpg | caption = The ''[[honden]]'', or main shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|33|23|22.09|N|130|33|47.52|E|region:JP-40_type:landmark_source:kolossus-dewiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[The Cowherd and the Weaver Girl|Orihime]]
[[Himekoso-no-Kami]] | founded_by = | established = Unknown | date_destroyed = | location = 1-1, Ōzaki, [[Ogōri, Fukuoka|Ogōri]]
[[Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka]] 838-0127 | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Tanabata-jinja'''|七夕神社|}}, also known as Himekoso-jinja (媛社神社), is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ogōri, Fukuoka|Ogōri]], [[Fukuoka prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is dedicated to Orihime, the Japanese name of the Weaver Girl from the Chinese folk tale [[The Cowherd and the Weaver Girl|The Weaver Girl and the Cowherd]].{{cite web|url=https://goraifuku.jp/spots/detail/18|script-title=ja:媛社神社（七夕神社）|trans-title=Himekoso-jinja|accessdate=September 18, 2019|publisher=[[Fukuoka Prefecture]]|language=Japanese}} The weaver is celebrated in [[Tanabata]], a Japanese festival. ==History== According to the Hizen-Fudoki, an ancient record of the [[Hizen Province]], this shrine was extant in 730. The original object of worship at the shrine was a river, Yamaji-gawa, on the south of the shrine. In ''[[Engishiki]]'', a formal record on shrines written 1000 years ago, this area was developed by the textile industry. The villagers believe the weaving goddess known as ''Tanabata-tsume'' (棚機津女) from ancient times, and the story of the Cowherd and the Weaver Girl from [[China]] was assimilated, it is now known as the god of textiles. ==Festivals== Tanabata Grand festival, the annual summer festival held August 6-8 every year.{{cite web|url=http://ogorishinorekishi.ogori.net/小郡市の歴史遺産/七夕神社/|script-title=ja:小郡市の歴史遺産 七夕神社|trans-title=Tanabata-jinja, the historical site in Ogōri city|accessdate=September 18, 2019|publisher=[[ogori city]]|language=Japanese}} On the evening of August 6 children bring the ''[[mikoshi]]'' from the shrine, carry it around the neighborhoods that worship at the shrine. At 5 o'clock on the following day morning, the lion dance is performed as a prayer dedicated to the shrine. And later in the morning, the lion dancer and children pulling a float visit neighbors. In the evening, many open-air stalls line the path leading to the shrine, and wishes written on strips of paper sent from people throughout Japan decorate every available space.{{cite web |url=https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/en/event/?mode=detail&id=4000000002698&isSpot=&isEvent=1|title=Summer Festival at Tanabata Shrine|publisher=[[Fukuoka Prefecture]] |accessdate=19 September 2019}} ==Gallery== File:Tanabata-jinja, Ogori, Fukuoka 01.jpg|Torii at Tanabata Shrine entrance. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== *[https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/en/event/?mode=detail&id=4000000000656 Tanabata Shrine - Fukuoka Prefecture Tourist Information] {{in lang|en}} {{Commons Category:Pages with redlinks to [[Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka]] 838-0127 | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Tanabata-jinja'''|七夕神社|}}, also known as Himekoso-jinja (媛社神社), is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ogōri, Fukuoka|Ogōri]], [[Fukuoka prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is dedicated to Orihime, the Japanese name of the Weaver Girl from the Chinese folk tale [[The Cowherd and the Weaver Girl|The Weaver Girl and the Cowherd]].{{cite web|url=https://goraifuku.jp/spots/detail/18|script-title=ja:媛社神社（七夕神社）|trans-title=Himekoso-jinja|accessdate=September 18, 2019|publisher=[[Fukuoka Prefecture]]|language=Japanese}} The weaver is celebrated in [[Tanabata]], a Japanese festival. ==History== According to the Hizen-Fudoki, an ancient record of the [[Hizen Province]], this shrine was extant in 730. The original object of worship at the shrine was a river, Yamaji-gawa, on the south of the shrine. In ''[[Engishiki]]'', a formal record on shrines written 1000 years ago, this area was developed by the textile industry. The villagers believe the weaving goddess known as ''Tanabata-tsume'' (棚機津女) from ancient times, and the story of the Cowherd and the Weaver Girl from [[China]] was assimilated, it is now known as the god of textiles. ==Festivals== Tanabata Grand festival, the annual summer festival held August 6-8 every year.{{cite web|url=http://ogorishinorekishi.ogori.net/小郡市の歴史遺産/七夕神社/|script-title=ja:小郡市の歴史遺産 七夕神社|trans-title=Tanabata-jinja, the historical site in Ogōri city|accessdate=September 18, 2019|publisher=[[ogori city]]|language=Japanese}} On the evening of August 6 children bring the ''[[mikoshi]]'' from the shrine, carry it around the neighborhoods that worship at the shrine. At 5 o'clock on the following day morning, the lion dance is performed as a prayer dedicated to the shrine. And later in the morning, the lion dancer and children pulling a float visit neighbors. In the evening, many open-air stalls line the path leading to the shrine, and wishes written on strips of paper sent from people throughout Japan decorate every available space.{{cite web |url=https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/en/event/?mode=detail&id=4000000002698&isSpot=&isEvent=1|title=Summer Festival at Tanabata Shrine|publisher=[[Fukuoka Prefecture]] |accessdate=19 September 2019}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Tanabata-jinja
七夕神社 | image = Tanabata-jinja, Ogori, Fukuoka_03.jpg | caption = The ''[[honden]]'', or main shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|33|23|22.09|N|130|33|47.52|E|region:JP-40_type:landmark_source:kolossus-dewiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[The Cowherd and the Weaver Girl|Orihime]]
[[Himekoso-no-Kami]] | founded_by = | established = Unknown | date_destroyed = | location = 1-1, Ōzaki, [[Ogōri, Fukuoka|Ogōri]]
[[Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka]] 838-0127 | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Tanabata-jinja'''|七夕神社|}}, also known as Himekoso-jinja (媛社神社), is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ogōri, Fukuoka|Ogōri]], [[Fukuoka prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is dedicated to Orihime, the Japanese name of the Weaver Girl from the Chinese folk tale [[The Cowherd and the Weaver Girl|The Weaver Girl and the Cowherd]].{{cite web|url=https://goraifuku.jp/spots/detail/18|script-title=ja:媛社神社（七夕神社）|trans-title=Himekoso-jinja|accessdate=September 18, 2019|publisher=[[Fukuoka Prefecture]]|language=Japanese}} The weaver is celebrated in [[Tanabata]], a Japanese festival. ==History== According to the Hizen-Fudoki, an ancient record of the [[Hizen Province]], this shrine was extant in 730. The original object of worship at the shrine was a river, Yamaji-gawa, on the south of the shrine. In ''[[Engishiki]]'', a formal record on shrines written 1000 years ago, this area was developed by the textile industry. The villagers believe the weaving goddess known as ''Tanabata-tsume'' (棚機津女) from ancient times, and the story of the Cowherd and the Weaver Girl from [[China]] was assimilated, it is now known as the god of textiles. ==Festivals== Tanabata Grand festival, the annual summer festival held August 6-8 every year.{{cite web|url=http://ogorishinorekishi.ogori.net/小郡市の歴史遺産/七夕神社/|script-title=ja:小郡市の歴史遺産 七夕神社|trans-title=Tanabata-jinja, the historical site in Ogōri city|accessdate=September 18, 2019|publisher=[[ogori city]]|language=Japanese}} On the evening of August 6 children bring the ''[[mikoshi]]'' from the shrine, carry it around the neighborhoods that worship at the shrine. At 5 o'clock on the following day morning, the lion dance is performed as a prayer dedicated to the shrine. And later in the morning, the lion dancer and children pulling a float visit neighbors. In the evening, many open-air stalls line the path leading to the shrine, and wishes written on strips of paper sent from people throughout Japan decorate every available space.{{cite web |url=https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/en/event/?mode=detail&id=4000000002698&isSpot=&isEvent=1|title=Summer Festival at Tanabata Shrine|publisher=[[Fukuoka Prefecture]] |accessdate=19 September 2019}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Tanabata-jinja
七夕神社 | image = Tanabata-jinja, Ogori, Fukuoka_03.jpg | caption = The ''[[honden]]'', or main shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|33|23|22.09|N|130|33|47.52|E|region:JP-40_type:landmark_source:kolossus-dewiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[The Cowherd and the Weaver Girl|Orihime]]
[[Himekoso-no-Kami]] | founded_by = | established = Unknown | date_destroyed = | location = 1-1, Ōzaki, [[Ogōri, Fukuoka|Ogōri]]
[[Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka]] 838-0127 | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Tanabata-jinja'''|七夕神社|}}, also known as Himekoso-jinja (媛社神社), is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ogōri, Fukuoka|Ogōri]], [[Fukuoka prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is dedicated to Orihime, the Japanese name of the Weaver Girl from the Chinese folk tale [[The Cowherd and the Weaver Girl|The Weaver Girl and the Cowherd]].{{cite web|url=https://goraifuku.jp/spots/detail/18|script-title=ja:媛社神社（七夕神社）|trans-title=Himekoso-jinja|accessdate=September 18, 2019|publisher=[[Fukuoka Prefecture]]|language=Japanese}} The weaver is celebrated in [[Tanabata]], a Japanese festival. ==History== According to the Hizen-Fudoki, an ancient record of the [[Hizen Province]], this shrine was extant in 730. The original object of worship at the shrine was a river, Yamaji-gawa, on the south of the shrine. In ''[[Engishiki]]'', a formal record on shrines written 1000 years ago, this area was developed by the textile industry. The villagers believe the weaving goddess known as ''Tanabata-tsume'' (棚機津女) from ancient times, and the story of the Cowherd and the Weaver Girl from [[China]] was assimilated, it is now known as the god of textiles. ==Festivals== Tanabata Grand festival, the annual summer festival held August 6-8 every year.{{cite web|url=http://ogorishinorekishi.ogori.net/小郡市の歴史遺産/七夕神社/|script-title=ja:小郡市の歴史遺産 七夕神社|trans-title=Tanabata-jinja, the historical site in Ogōri city|accessdate=September 18, 2019|publisher=[[ogori city]]|language=Japanese}} On the evening of August 6 children bring the ''[[mikoshi]]'' from the shrine, carry it around the neighborhoods that worship at the shrine. At 5 o'clock on the following day morning, the lion dance is performed as a prayer dedicated to the shrine. And later in the morning, the lion dancer and children pulling a float visit neighbors. In the evening, many open-air stalls line the path leading to the shrine, and wishes written on strips of paper sent from people throughout Japan decorate every available space.{{cite web |url=https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/en/event/?mode=detail&id=4000000002698&isSpot=&isEvent=1|title=Summer Festival at Tanabata Shrine|publisher=[[Fukuoka Prefecture]] |accessdate=19 September 2019}} ==Gallery== File:Tanabata-jinja, Ogori, Fukuoka 01.jpg|Torii at Tanabata Shrine entrance. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== *[https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/en/event/?mode=detail&id=4000000000656 Tanabata Shrine - Fukuoka Prefecture Tourist Information] {{in lang|en}} {{Commons Category:Pages with redlinks to It is dedicated to Orihime, the Japanese name of the Weaver Girl from the Chinese folk tale [[The Cowherd and the Weaver Girl|The Weaver Girl and the Cowherd]].{{cite web|url=https://goraifuku.jp/spots/detail/18|script-title=ja:媛社神社（七夕神社）|trans-title=Himekoso-jinja|accessdate=September 18, 2019|publisher=[[Fukuoka Prefecture]]|language=Japanese}} The weaver is celebrated in [[Tanabata]], a Japanese festival. ==History== According to the Hizen-Fudoki, an ancient record of the [[Hizen Province]], this shrine was extant in 730. The original object of worship at the shrine was a river, Yamaji-gawa, on the south of the shrine. In ''[[Engishiki]]'', a formal record on shrines written 1000 years ago, this area was developed by the textile industry. The villagers believe the weaving goddess known as ''Tanabata-tsume'' (棚機津女) from ancient times, and the story of the Cowherd and the Weaver Girl from [[China]] was assimilated, it is now known as the god of textiles. ==Festivals== Tanabata Grand festival, the annual summer festival held August 6-8 every year.{{cite web|url=http://ogorishinorekishi.ogori.net/小郡市の歴史遺産/七夕神社/|script-title=ja:小郡市の歴史遺産 七夕神社|trans-title=Tanabata-jinja, the historical site in Ogōri city|accessdate=September 18, 2019|publisher=[[ogori city]]|language=Japanese}} On the evening of August 6 children bring the ''[[mikoshi]]'' from the shrine, carry it around the neighborhoods that worship at the shrine. At 5 o'clock on the following day morning, the lion dance is performed as a prayer dedicated to the shrine. And later in the morning, the lion dancer and children pulling a float visit neighbors. In the evening, many open-air stalls line the path leading to the shrine, and wishes written on strips of paper sent from people throughout Japan decorate every available space.{{cite web |url=https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/en/event/?mode=detail&id=4000000002698&isSpot=&isEvent=1|title=Summer Festival at Tanabata Shrine|publisher=[[Fukuoka Prefecture]] |accessdate=19 September 2019}} ==Gallery== File:Tanabata-jinja, Ogori, Fukuoka 01.jpg|Torii at Tanabata Shrine entrance. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== *[https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/en/event/?mode=detail&id=4000000000656 Tanabata Shrine - Fukuoka Prefecture Tourist Information] {{in lang|en}} {{Commons Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category|Tanabata-jinja_(Fukuoka)|Tanabata Shrine}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category: tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Tanabata Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Tourist attractions in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Completed pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-27]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Tanabata Shrine]] [[en:Tanabata Shrine]]

The original object of worship at the shrine was a river, Yamaji-gawa, on the south of the shrine. In ''[[Engishiki]]'', a formal record on shrines written 1000 years ago, this area was developed by the textile industry. [[Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka]] 838-0127 | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Tanabata-jinja'''|七夕神社|}}, also known as Himekoso-jinja (媛社神社), is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ogōri, Fukuoka|Ogōri]], [[Fukuoka prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is dedicated to Orihime, the Japanese name of the Weaver Girl from the Chinese folk tale [[The Cowherd and the Weaver Girl|The Weaver Girl and the Cowherd]].{{cite web|url=https://goraifuku.jp/spots/detail/18|script-title=ja:媛社神社（七夕神社）|trans-title=Himekoso-jinja|accessdate=September 18, 2019|publisher=[[Fukuoka Prefecture]]|language=Japanese}} The weaver is celebrated in [[Tanabata]], a Japanese festival. ==History== According to the Hizen-Fudoki, an ancient record of the [[Hizen Province]], this shrine was extant in 730. The original object of worship at the shrine was a river, Yamaji-gawa, on the south of the shrine. In ''[[Engishiki]]'', a formal record on shrines written 1000 years ago, this area was developed by the textile industry. The villagers believe the weaving goddess known as ''Tanabata-tsume'' (棚機津女) from ancient times, and the story of the Cowherd and the Weaver Girl from [[China]] was assimilated, it is now known as the god of textiles. ==Festivals== Tanabata Grand festival, the annual summer festival held August 6-8 every year.{{cite web|url=http://ogorishinorekishi.ogori.net/小郡市の歴史遺産/七夕神社/|script-title=ja:小郡市の歴史遺産 七夕神社|trans-title=Tanabata-jinja, the historical site in Ogōri city|accessdate=September 18, 2019|publisher=[[ogori city]]|language=Japanese}} On the evening of August 6 children bring the ''[[mikoshi]]'' from the shrine, carry it around the neighborhoods that worship at the shrine. At 5 o'clock on the following day morning, the lion dance is performed as a prayer dedicated to the shrine. And later in the morning, the lion dancer and children pulling a float visit neighbors. In the evening, many open-air stalls line the path leading to the shrine, and wishes written on strips of paper sent from people throughout Japan decorate every available space.{{cite web |url=https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/en/event/?mode=detail&id=4000000002698&isSpot=&isEvent=1|title=Summer Festival at Tanabata Shrine|publisher=[[Fukuoka Prefecture]] |accessdate=19 September 2019}} ==Gallery== File:Tanabata-jinja, Ogori, Fukuoka 01.jpg|Torii at Tanabata Shrine entrance. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== *[https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/en/event/?mode=detail&id=4000000000656 Tanabata Shrine - Fukuoka Prefecture Tourist Information] {{in lang|en}} {{Commons Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category|Tanabata-jinja_(Fukuoka)|Tanabata Shrine}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Tanabata Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Tourist attractions in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Completed pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Tanabata Shrine]] [[en:Tanabata Shrine]]

In the evening, many open-air stalls line the path leading to the shrine, and wishes written on strips of paper sent from people throughout Japan decorate every available space.{{cite web |url=https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/en/event/?mode=detail&id=4000000002698&isSpot=&isEvent=1|title=Summer Festival at Tanabata Shrine|publisher=[[Fukuoka Prefecture]] |accessdate=19 September 2019}} ==Gallery== File:Tanabata-jinja, Ogori, Fukuoka 01.jpg|Torii at Tanabata Shrine entrance. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== *[https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/en/event/?mode=detail&id=4000000000656 Tanabata Shrine - Fukuoka Prefecture Tourist Information] {{in lang|en}} {{Commons Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category|Tanabata-jinja_(Fukuoka)|Tanabata Shrine}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Cgoodbye $1 1]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Tanabata Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Tourist attractions in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Completed pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tanabata Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Tanabata Shrine]] [[en:Tanabata Shrine]]

[[Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka]] 838-0127 | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Tanabata-jinja'''|七夕神社|}}, also known as Himekoso-jinja (媛社神社), is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ogōri, Fukuoka|Ogōri]], [[Fukuoka prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The original object of worship at the shrine was a river, Yamaji-gawa, on the south of the shrine. At 5 o'clock on the following day morning, the lion dance is performed as a prayer dedicated to the shrine. And later in the morning, the lion dancer and children pulling a float visit neighbors. It is dedicated to Orihime, the Japanese name of the Weaver Girl from the Chinese folk tale [[The Cowherd and the Weaver Girl|The Weaver Girl and the Cowherd]].{{cite web|url=https://goraifuku.jp/spots/detail/18|script-title=ja:媛社神社（七夕神社）|trans-title=Himekoso-jinja|accessdate=September 18, 2019|publisher=[[Fukuoka Prefecture]]|language=Japanese}} The weaver is celebrated in [[Tanabata]], a Japanese festival. ==History== According to the Hizen-Fudoki, an ancient record of the [[Hizen Province]], this shrine was extant in 730. The original object of worship at the shrine was a river, Yamaji-gawa, on the south of the shrine. In ''[[Engishiki]]'', a formal record on shrines written 1000 years ago, this area was developed by the textile industry. The villagers believe the weaving goddess known as ''Tanabata-tsume'' (棚機津女) from ancient times, and the story of the Cowherd and the Weaver Girl from [[China]] was assimilated, it is now known as the god of textiles. ==Festivals== Tanabata Grand festival, the annual summer festival held August 6-8 every year.{{cite web|url=http://ogorishinorekishi.ogori.net/小郡市の歴史遺産/七夕神社/|script-title=ja:小郡市の歴史遺産 七夕神社|trans-title=Tanabata-jinja, the historical site in Ogōri city|accessdate=September 18, 2019|publisher=[[ogori city]]|language=Japanese}} On the evening of August 6 children bring the ''[[mikoshi]]'' from the shrine, carry it around the neighborhoods that worship at the shrine. At 5 o'clock on the following day morning, the lion dance is performed as a prayer dedicated to the shrine. And later in the morning, the lion dancer and children pulling a float visit neighbors. In the evening, many open-air stalls line the path leading to the shrine, and wishes written on strips of paper sent from people throughout Japan decorate every available space.{{cite web |url=https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/en/event/?mode=detail&id=4000000002698&isSpot=&isEvent=1|title=Summer Festival at Tanabata Shrine|publisher=[[Fukuoka Prefecture]] |accessdate=19 September 2019}} ==Gallery== File:Tanabata-jinja, Ogori, Fukuoka 01.jpg|Torii at Tanabata Shrine entrance. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== *[https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/en/event/?mode=detail&id=4000000000656 Tanabata Shrine - Fukuoka Prefecture Tourist Information] {{in lang|en}} {{Commons Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category|Tanabata-jinja_(Fukuoka)|Tanabata Shrine}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Cgoodbye $1 1]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Tourist attractions in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Completed pages from Wikipedia]] [[sn:Tanabata Shrine]]}} [[sn:Tanabata Shrine]] [[en:Tanabata Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis]] {{Commons category|Tanabata-jinja (Fukuoka)}} [[fr:Tanabata-jinja]] [[ja:七夕神社]]

In the evening, many open-air stalls line the path leading to the shrine, and wishes written on strips of paper sent from people throughout Japan decorate every available space.{{cite web |url=https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/en/event/?mode=detail&id=4000000002698&isSpot=&isEvent=1|title=Summer Festival at Tanabata Shrine|publisher=[[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] |accessdate=19 September 2019}} ==Gallery== File:Tanabata-jinja, Ogori, Fukuoka 01.jpg|Torii at Tanabata Shrine entrance. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== *[https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/en/event/?mode=detail&id=4000000000656 Tanabata Shrine - Fukuoka Prefecture Tourist Information] {{in lang|en}} {{Commons Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category|Tanabata-jinja_(Fukuoka)|Tanabata Shrine}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Tourist attractions in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[sn:Tanabata Shrine]]}} [[sn:Tanabata Shrine]] [[en:Tanabata Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis]] {{Commons category|Tanabata-jinja (Fukuoka)}} [[fr:Tanabata-jinja]] [[ja:七夕神社]]

{{Infobox religious building | name = Tanabata-jinja
七夕神社 | image = Tanabata-jinja, Ogori, Fukuoka_03.jpg | caption = The ''[[honden]]'', or main shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|33|23|22.09|N|130|33|47.52|E|region:JP-40_type:landmark_source:kolossus-dewiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[The Cowherd and the Weaver Girl|Orihime]]
[[Himekoso-no-Kami]] | founded_by = | established = Unknown | date_destroyed = | location = 1-1, Ōzaki, [[Ogōri, Fukuoka|Ogōri]]
[[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka]] 838-0127 | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Tanabata-jinja'''|七夕神社|}}, also known as Himekoso-jinja (媛社神社), is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ogōri, Fukuoka|Ogōri]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is dedicated to Orihime, the Japanese name of the Weaver Girl from the Chinese folk tale [[The Cowherd and the Weaver Girl|The Weaver Girl and the Cowherd]].{{cite web|url=https://goraifuku.jp/spots/detail/18|script-title=ja:媛社神社（七夕神社）|trans-title=Himekoso-jinja|accessdate=September 18, 2019|publisher=[[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]|language=Japanese}} The weaver is celebrated in [[Tanabata]], a Japanese festival. ==History== According to the Hizen-Fudoki, an ancient record of the [[:en:Hizen Province|Hizen Province]], this shrine was extant in 730. The original object of worship at the shrine was a river, Yamaji-gawa, on the south of the shrine. In ''[[Engishiki]]'', a formal record on shrines written 1000 years ago, this area was developed by the textile industry. The villagers believe the weaving goddess known as ''Tanabata-tsume'' (棚機津女) from ancient times, and the story of the Cowherd and the Weaver Girl from [[:en:China|China]] was assimilated, it is now known as the god of textiles. ==Festivals== Tanabata Grand festival, the annual summer festival held August 6-8 every year.{{cite web|url=http://ogorishinorekishi.ogori.net/小郡の Municipal History遺産/七夕神社/|script-title=ja:小郡の Municipal History遺産 七夕神社|trans-title=Tanabata-jinja, the historical site in Ogōri city|accessdate=September 18, 2019|publisher=[[ogori city]]|language=Japanese}} On the evening of August 6 children bring the ''[[mikoshi]]'' from the shrine, carry it around the neighborhoods that worship at the shrine. At 5 o'clock on the following day morning, the lion dance is performed as a prayer dedicated to the shrine. And later in the morning, the lion dancer and children pulling a float visit neighbors. In the evening, many open-air stalls line the path leading to the shrine, and wishes written on strips of paper sent from people throughout Japan decorate every available space.{{cite web |url=https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/en/event/?mode=detail&id=4000000002698&isSpot=&isEvent=1|title=Summer Festival at Tanabata Shrine|publisher=[[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] |accessdate=19 September 2019}} ==Gallery== File:Tanabata-jinja, Ogori, Fukuoka 01.jpg|Torii at Tanabata Shrine entrance. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== *[https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/en/event/?mode=detail&id=4000000000656 Tanabata Shrine - Fukuoka Prefecture Tourist Information] {{in lang|en}} {{Commons Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category|Tanabata-jinja_(Fukuoka)|Tanabata Shrine}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Tourist attractions in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[sn:Tanabata Shrine]]}} [[sn:Tanabata Shrine]] [[en:Tanabata Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis]] {{Commons category|Tanabata-jinja (Fukuoka)}} [[fr:Tanabata-jinja]] [[ja:七夕神社]]

[[Himekoso-no-Kami]] | founded_by = | established = Unknown | date_destroyed = | location = 1-1, Ōzaki, [[:en:Ogōri, Fukuoka|Ogōri]]
[[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka]] 838-0127 | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | location = {{ill|Ogori|WD=Q683248}} }} {{Nihongo|'''Tanabata-jinja'''|七夕神社|}}, also known as Himekoso-jinja (媛社神社), is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Ogōri, Fukuoka|Ogōri]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is dedicated to Orihime, the Japanese name of the Weaver Girl from the Chinese folk tale [[:en:The Cowherd and the Weaver Girl|The Weaver Girl and the Cowherd]].{{cite web|url=https://goraifuku.jp/spots/detail/18|script-title=ja:媛社神社（七夕神社）|trans-title=Himekoso-jinja|accessdate=September 18, 2019|publisher=[[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]|language=Japanese}} The weaver is celebrated in [[:en:Tanabata|Tanabata]], a Japanese festival. ==History== According to the Hizen-Fudoki, an ancient record of the [[:en:Hizen Province|Hizen Province]], this shrine was extant in 730. The original object of worship at the shrine was a river, Yamaji-gawa, on the south of the shrine. In ''[[Engishiki]]'', a formal record on shrines written 1000 years ago, this area was developed by the textile industry. The villagers believe the weaving goddess known as ''Tanabata-tsume'' (棚機津女) from ancient times, and the story of the Cowherd and the Weaver Girl from [[:en:China|China]] was assimilated, it is now known as the god of textiles. ==Festivals== Tanabata Grand festival, the annual summer festival held August 6-8 every year.{{cite web|url=http://ogorishinorekishi.ogori.net/小郡の Municipal History遺産/七夕神社/|script-title=ja:小郡の Municipal History遺産 七夕神社|trans-title=Tanabata-jinja, the historical site in Ogōri city|accessdate=September 18, 2019|publisher=[[ogori city]]|language=Japanese}} On the evening of August 6 children bring the ''[[mikoshi]]'' from the shrine, carry it around the neighborhoods that worship at the shrine. At 5 o'clock on the following day morning, the lion dance is performed as a prayer dedicated to the shrine. And later in the morning, the lion dancer and children pulling a float visit neighbors. In the evening, many open-air stalls line the path leading to the shrine, and wishes written on strips of paper sent from people throughout Japan decorate every available space.{{cite web |url=https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/en/event/?mode=detail&id=4000000002698&isSpot=&isEvent=1|title=Summer Festival at Tanabata Shrine|publisher=[[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] |accessdate=19 September 2019}} ==Gallery== File:Tanabata-jinja, Ogori, Fukuoka 01.jpg|Torii at Tanabata Shrine entrance. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== *[https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/en/event/?mode=detail&id=4000000000656 Tanabata Shrine - Fukuoka Prefecture Tourist Information] {{in lang|en}} {{Commons Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category|Tanabata-jinja_(Fukuoka)|Tanabata Shrine}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[en:Tanabata Shrine]] {{Commons category|Tanabata-jinja (Fukuoka)}} [[fr:Tanabata-jinja]] [[ja:七夕神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11353446}} [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Tourist attractions in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[co:commons:Category:Tanabata-jinja (Fukuoka)]][[en:Tanabata Shrine]][[fr:Tanabata-jinja]][[ja:七夕神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Tanabata-jinja (Fukuoka)]][[en:Tanabata Shrine]][[fr:Tanabata-jinja]][[ja:七夕神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Tanabata-jinja (Fukuoka)]][[en:Tanabata Shrine]][[fr:Tanabata-jinja]][[ja:七夕神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11353446]] Wikidata name: Tanabata-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:50, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Tanabata-jinja
七夕神社 | image = File:Tanabata-jinja, Ogori, Fukuoka 03.jpg | caption = The ''[[honden]]'', or main shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|33.38947|N|130.5632|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|The Cowherd and the Weaver Girl|en|The Cowherd and the Weaver Girl|WD=Q910146|lt=Orihime}}
[[Himekoso-no-Kami]] | founded_by = | established = Unknown | date_destroyed = | location = 1-1, Ōzaki, {{ill|Ogōri, Fukuoka|en|Ogōri, Fukuoka|WD=Q683248|lt=Ogōri}}
{{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka}} 838-0127 | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | location = {{ill|Ogori|WD=Q683248}} }} {{Nihongo|'''Tanabata-jinja'''|七夕神社|}}, also known as Himekoso-jinja (媛社神社), is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Ogōri, Fukuoka|en|Ogōri, Fukuoka|WD=Q683248|lt=Ogōri}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. It is dedicated to Orihime, the Japanese name of the Weaver Girl from the Chinese folk tale {{ill|The Cowherd and the Weaver Girl|en|The Cowherd and the Weaver Girl|WD=Q910146|lt=The Weaver Girl and the Cowherd}}.{{cite web|url=https://goraifuku.jp/spots/detail/18|script-title=ja:媛社神社（七夕神社）|trans-title=Himekoso-jinja|accessdate=September 18, 2019|publisher={{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture}}|language=Japanese}} The weaver is celebrated in {{ill|Tanabata|en|Tanabata|WD=Q1193996|lt=Tanabata}}, a Japanese festival. ==History== According to the Hizen-Fudoki, an ancient record of the {{ill|Hizen Province|en|Hizen Province|WD=Q906926|lt=Hizen Province}}, this shrine was extant in 730. The original object of worship at the shrine was a river, Yamaji-gawa, on the south of the shrine. In ''[[Engishiki]]'', a formal record on shrines written 1000 years ago, this area was developed by the textile industry. The villagers believe the weaving goddess known as ''Tanabata-tsume'' (棚機津女) from ancient times, and the story of the Cowherd and the Weaver Girl from {{ill|China|en|China|WD=Q148|lt=China}} was assimilated, it is now known as the god of textiles. ==Festivals== Tanabata Grand festival, the annual summer festival held August 6-8 every year.{{cite web|url=http://ogorishinorekishi.ogori.net/小郡の Municipal History遺産/七夕神社/|script-title=ja:小郡の Municipal History遺産 七夕神社|trans-title=Tanabata-jinja, the historical site in Ogōri city|accessdate=September 18, 2019|publisher=[[ogori city]]|language=Japanese}} On the evening of August 6 children bring the ''[[mikoshi]]'' from the shrine, carry it around the neighborhoods that worship at the shrine. At 5 o'clock on the following day morning, the lion dance is performed as a prayer dedicated to the shrine. And later in the morning, the lion dancer and children pulling a float visit neighbors. In the evening, many open-air stalls line the path leading to the shrine, and wishes written on strips of paper sent from people throughout Japan decorate every available space.{{cite web |url=https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/en/event/?mode=detail&id=4000000002698&isSpot=&isEvent=1|title=Summer Festival at Tanabata Shrine|publisher={{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture}} |accessdate=19 September 2019}} ==Gallery== File:Tanabata-jinja, Ogori, Fukuoka 01.jpg|Torii at Tanabata Shrine entrance. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== *[https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/en/event/?mode=detail&id=4000000000656 Tanabata Shrine - Fukuoka Prefecture Tourist Information] {{in lang|en}} {{Commons Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category|Tanabata-jinja_(Fukuoka)|Tanabata Shrine}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[en:Tanabata Shrine]] {{Commons category|Tanabata-jinja (Fukuoka)}} [[fr:Tanabata-jinja]] [[ja:七夕神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11353446}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Tourist attractions in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]]

The villagers believe the weaving goddess known as ''Tanabata-tsume'' (棚機津女) from ancient times, and the story of the Cowherd and the Weaver Girl from {{ill|China|en|China|WD=Q148|lt=China|1=China}} was assimilated, it is now known as the god of textiles. ==Festivals== Tanabata Grand festival, the annual summer festival held August 6-8 every year.{{cite web|url=http://ogorishinorekishi.ogori.net/小郡の Municipal History遺産/七夕神社/|script-title=ja:小郡の Municipal History遺産 七夕神社|trans-title=Tanabata-jinja, the historical site in Ogōri city|accessdate=September 18, 2019|publisher=[[ogori city]]|language=Japanese}} On the evening of August 6 children bring the ''[[mikoshi]]'' from the shrine, carry it around the neighborhoods that worship at the shrine. At 5 o'clock on the following day morning, the lion dance is performed as a prayer dedicated to the shrine. And later in the morning, the lion dancer and children pulling a float visit neighbors. In the evening, many open-air stalls line the path leading to the shrine, and wishes written on strips of paper sent from people throughout Japan decorate every available space.{{cite web |url=https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/en/event/?mode=detail&id=4000000002698&isSpot=&isEvent=1|title=Summer Festival at Tanabata Shrine|publisher={{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}} |accessdate=19 September 2019}} ==Gallery== File:Tanabata-jinja, Ogori, Fukuoka 01.jpg|Torii at Tanabata Shrine entrance. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== *[https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/en/event/?mode=detail&id=4000000000656 Tanabata Shrine - Fukuoka Prefecture Tourist Information] {{in lang|en}} {{Commons Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category|Tanabata-jinja_(Fukuoka)|Tanabata Shrine}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[en:Tanabata Shrine]] {{Commons category|Tanabata-jinja (Fukuoka)}} [[fr:Tanabata-jinja]] [[ja:七夕神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11353446}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Tourist attractions in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:pages]]

{{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}} 838-0127 | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | location = {{ill|Ogori|WD=Q683248|1=Ogōri, Fukuoka}} }} {{Nihongo|'''Tanabata-jinja'''|七夕神社|}}, also known as Himekoso-jinja (媛社神社), is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Ogōri, Fukuoka|en|Ogōri, Fukuoka|WD=Q683248|lt=Ogōri|1=Ogōri, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. It is dedicated to Orihime, the Japanese name of the Weaver Girl from the Chinese folk tale {{ill|The Cowherd and the Weaver Girl|en|The Cowherd and the Weaver Girl|WD=Q910146|lt=The Weaver Girl and the Cowherd|1=The Cowherd and the Weaver Girl}}.{{cite web|url=https://goraifuku.jp/spots/detail/18|script-title=ja:媛社神社（七夕神社）|trans-title=Himekoso-jinja|accessdate=September 18, 2019|publisher={{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}|language=Japanese}} The weaver is celebrated in {{ill|Tanabata|en|Tanabata|WD=Q1193996|lt=Tanabata|1=Tanabata}}, a Japanese festival. ==History== According to the Hizen-Fudoki, an ancient record of the {{ill|Hizen Province|en|Hizen Province|WD=Q906926|lt=Hizen Province|1=Hizen Province}}, this shrine was extant in 730. The original object of worship at the shrine was a river, Yamaji-gawa, on the south of the shrine. In ''[[Engishiki]]'', a formal record on shrines written 1000 years ago, this area was developed by the textile industry. The villagers believe the weaving goddess known as ''Tanabata-tsume'' (棚機津女) from ancient times, and the story of the Cowherd and the Weaver Girl from {{ill|China|en|China|WD=Q148|lt=China|1=China}} was assimilated, it is now known as the god of textiles. ==Festivals== Tanabata Grand festival, the annual summer festival held August 6-8 every year.{{cite web|url=http://ogorishinorekishi.ogori.net/小郡の Municipal History遺産/七夕神社/|script-title=ja:小郡の Municipal History遺産 七夕神社|trans-title=Tanabata-jinja, the historical site in Ogōri city|accessdate=September 18, 2019|publisher=[[ogori city]]|language=Japanese}} On the evening of August 6 children bring the ''[[mikoshi]]'' from the shrine, carry it around the neighborhoods that worship at the shrine. At 5 o'clock on the following day morning, the lion dance is performed as a prayer dedicated to the shrine. And later in the morning, the lion dancer and children pulling a float visit neighbors. In the evening, many open-air stalls line the path leading to the shrine, and wishes written on strips of paper sent from people throughout Japan decorate every available space.{{cite web |url=https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/en/event/?mode=detail&id=4000000002698&isSpot=&isEvent=1|title=Summer Festival at Tanabata Shrine|publisher={{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}} |accessdate=19 September 2019}} ==Gallery== File:Tanabata-jinja, Ogori, Fukuoka 01.jpg|Torii at Tanabata Shrine entrance. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== *[https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/en/event/?mode=detail&id=4000000000656 Tanabata Shrine - Fukuoka Prefecture Tourist Information] {{in lang|en}} {{Commons Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category|Tanabata-jinja_(Fukuoka)|Tanabata Shrine}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Commons category|Tanabata-jinja (Fukuoka)}} {{wikidata link|Q11353446|en|Tanabata Shrine|fr|Tanabata-jinja|ja|七夕神社}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Tourist attractions in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
